Ayahs

طسٓمٓ (۱)
Ta, Seen, Meem. (1)
تِلْكَ ءَايَـٰتُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ٱلْمُبِينِ (۲)
These are the signs of the Manifest Book. (2)
نَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْكَ مِن نَّبَإِ مُوسَىٰ وَفِرْعَوْنَ بِٱلْحَقِّ لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ (۳)
We relate to you truly some of the account of Moses and Pharaoh for a people who have faith. (3)

Messages

1- The Quran, the eternal miracle of Islam, is composed of these very Arabic alphabet letters. If you consider it the word of man, then produce something similar to it. «طسم، تلك آیات»

2- The Quran is a clear, transparent, and universally understandable Book. «الكتاب المبین»

3- Relating the history of the predecessors is a sign of the systematic nature of Divine traditions in history. If history lacked order, structure, and law, we could not use it for our lives today. «نتلوا... من نبأ موسى... لقوم یؤمنون»

4- When recounting history, we should pay attention to the important and instructive segments. (The word nabā’ refers to important news.)

5- The struggle of the Prophets against tyrants (ṭāghūt) and oppressors is a model for the believers. «نبأ موسى و فرعون... لقوم یؤمنون»

6- The stories in the Quran are not myths, fantasies, or exaggerations. «نبأ موسى و فرعون بالحقّ»

7- The Quran’s goal in relating stories is the guidance of the believers. «لقوم یؤمنون»

8- The first condition for guidance is receptivity. «لقوم یؤمنون»

Explanations

Introduction: «الحمد للّه ربّ العالمین و صلّى اللّه على سیدنا و نبیّنا محمّد و آله الطاهرین» In today's world, the enemies of humanity, using the most advanced tools, are attacking our beliefs, culture, ethics, and faith from land and sky, sparing no effort in conspiracy, distortion, and the creation of doubts. Trials (fitan) have cast shadows like dark clouds, and humanity's need for a secure ark has become greater than ever.

If man needs a guide, the Heavenly Book is the Imam and the guide (1). If man needs a teacher and educator, the Quran is the best guide (2). If society needs law and justice, the Quran is the book of justice and law (3). If man needs logic and proof, what proof is stronger than the Quran? (4) If man needs reminder and admonition, the Quran is the means of reminder and admonition (5). If man requires a remedy for spiritual ailments such as envy, arrogance, pride, greed, stinginess, selfishness, and hypocrisy, the Quran is the best healing medicine (6). The most important problem facing humanity today is various anxieties, stress, and worries, and the only means of tranquility is the remembrance of Allah, and the Quran is the best remembrance of Him (7).

If man needs hope, the Quran is full of hope. The Quran states: Allah protected Jonah beneath the water, Noah upon the water, and Joseph at the bottom of the well and beside the water. He can make dry land appear from a single strike of a staff upon the Nile River, and the same staff, when struck upon a rock elsewhere, can cause twelve springs of water to gush forth. He can drown and destroy Pharaoh in the water and Qarun in the earth, yet keep Abraham safe amidst the fire. Perhaps it is for this reason that there is a rebuke for not reflecting upon the Quran that does not exist for ignorance of other sciences (8).

The Quran is such that there are points of wisdom not only in every Surah, Ayah, and word but also in every letter. Imam Sadiq was asked: Why do you wipe only a part of your head during wudu (ablution) instead of the entire head? The Imam replied: Because of the letter bā’ (ب) in the Ayah «و امسحوا برؤسكم» (9). Yes, one must pay close attention to all the letters and words of the Quran and extract points of wisdom.

Every tafsir (exegesis) has been and is written with a specific methodology. Some have paid more attention to literary points, such as Tafsir al-Kashshaf; others have focused more on proposing various possibilities related to each word, such as Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr al-Razi. Some have primarily focused on quoting the opinions of other exegetes, such as Tafsir al-Qurtubi; some have given greater attention to social issues, such as Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Quran and Al-Manar. Some have only included narrations (riwāyāt) beneath each Ayah, such as the tafsirs of Safi, Burhan, Nur al-Thaqalayn, and Kanz al-Daqa’iq; some have exerted greater effort to interpret the verses of the Quran with the help of other verses, such as Tafsir al-Mizan; some have paid special attention to the natural issues discussed in the Quran, such as Tafsir Tantawi, and some have dedicated most of their effort to explaining the verses and presenting related discussions, such as Tafsir Namuneh.

However, in this tafsir, we have avoided quoting opinions useful only to specialists, as well as literary disputes that are not beneficial to the general public. Instead, effort has been made to extract practical points that are useful for people's thought or action in daily life.

Esteemed readers who have good education, if they reflect upon the points and messages of this tafsir, new ideas may occur to them that did not come to the author's mind. If they send those points to us, we will, while thanking them, include them in subsequent editions with a special mark.

Yes; according to the saying of Hadrat Ali, the Quran is a sea whose depth cannot be fathomed: «بحراً لایدك قعره» (10). Just as water is the source of life and the origin of all waters is the sea, the origin of all truths is Divine Revelation. Just as divers go to the depths of the sea and bring out pearls and coral, scholars also extract lofty concepts by delving deeply into the Quran. Entering the sea without knowing how to swim is dangerous, and entering the exegesis of the Quran without familiarity with the method of deriving meaning from it is also dangerous. Everyone benefits from the sea according to the size of their vessel. Likewise, everyone benefits from the boundless sea of the Quran according to their capacity.

Hoping for the day when the exegesis of the Quran is discussed more than other lessons in the seminaries (Ḥawzah). Hoping for the day when the exegesis of the Quran regains its special place in universities. Hoping for the day when the centrality of the Quran is evident in mosques, pulpits, and sermons. Hoping for the day when the recitation of the Quran is not merely for the opening of ceremonies but is a prelude to understanding it. The Quran states: "Do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated, until you know what you are saying" (11). Yes; recitation is a passage, not a stopping point.

By the grace of Allah, good steps have been taken for the Quran under the Islamic Republic system, and the exegesis of the Quran has seen significant growth in the religious seminaries. But this growth must expand and deepen in such a way that it becomes the foundation of our beliefs, ethics, economy, politics, individual, social, and family relations, and all other affairs of our lives, and we do not merely suffice with luxurious printing, beautiful binding, tilework, kissing it, seeking istikhara (guidance), swearing by it, or placing the Quran on our heads during the Nights of Qadr and when traveling.

Mohsen Qara'ati - Shahrivar 1382 (August/September 2003)

Portrait of Surah Al-Qasas

This Surah has eighty-eight verses and was revealed in Mecca before the Hijra (migration). Due to the appearance of the word qaṣaṣ in Ayah 25, this Surah has also been named Al-Qaṣaṣ, which means "the narration of history" or "the account."

Ayahs 3 to 46 of this Surah deal extensively with the story of Hadrat Moses and Pharaoh, and its concluding verses address the story of Qarun, referring to the destiny of the virtuous patient ones and the sinful corrupt ones. Although the story of Hadrat Moses and Pharaoh is mentioned in some way in thirty-four Surahs of the Holy Quran, it is only in this Surah that attention is paid to all aspects of his life, from his birth, childhood, and youth to his marriage, prophethood, and call to monotheism (12).

1) ان القرآن امام من الله». بحار، ج ۶۵، ص ۳۹۳. 2) هدى للمتقین» بقره، ۲. 3) و انّ القرآن... حكم عدل». بحار، ج ۲۲، ص ۳۱۵. 4) قد جائكم برهان من ربكم». نساء،۱۷۴ 5) قد جائتكم موعظة من ربكم». یونس،۵۷. 6) و ننزل من القرآن ما هو شفاء و رحمة». اسراء،۸۲. 7) نحن نزلنا الذكر». حجر،۹ «الا بذكر الله تطمئن القلوب». رعد،۲۸. 8) أفلایتدبرون القرآن أم على قلب اقفالها». محمد،۲۴ 9) مائده،۶. 10) نهج البلاغه، خطبه ۱۹۸. 11) نساء، ۴۳. 12) تفسیر فى ظلال القرآن.

Ayah

إِنَّ فِرْعَوْنَ عَلَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَجَعَلَ أَهْلَهَا شِيَعًا يَسْتَضْعِفُ طَآئِفَةً مِّنْهُمْ يُذَبِّحُ أَبْنَآءَهُمْ وَيَسْتَحْىِۦ نِسَآءَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ (٤)
Indeed Pharaoh tyrannized over the land, reducing its people to factions, abasing one group of them, slaughtering their sons, and sparing their women. Indeed, He was one of the agents of corruption. (4)

Key Points

The word "شِیَع" (shiya'), the plural of "شیعة" (shi'ah), originally means following and obedience. However, since in a group, some usually follow others, it also comes to mean a group. The word "نساء" (nisa') means women, but perhaps in this Ayah, it refers to girls, because they are placed in contrast to the boys. The word "فرعون" (Pharaoh) is not a personal name, but rather the title of the kings of Egypt. Just as "كسرى" (Chosroes) was the title of the kings of Persia and "قیصر" (Caesar) was the title of the kings of Rome.(13)

13) Dehkhoda Dictionary.

Messages

1- Understanding the conditions and circumstances of societies during the appearance and mission of the Prophets is a necessary matter. «اِنّ فرعون...»

2- In understanding regimes, individuals are not important; rather, performance matters. «انّ فرعون علا فى الارض» (The name "Pharaoh" is due to his arrogant and aggressive performance.)

3- Arrogance and tyranny are the grounds for corruption and sin. «علا فى الارض... یستضعف... یذبّح...»

4- Creating division is the most prominent lever of the arrogant ones' dominance over the people. «جعل اهلها شیعا» (As long as the people maintain unity and solidarity, tyrants have no power and cannot achieve anything.)

5- Division is the prelude to accepting humiliation. First, people become groups and scattered, and then they are subjected to subjugation (mustadh'af). «شیعاً یستضعف»

6- The unlawful actions of the agents are also counted against the ruler. In these verses, all the crimes of Pharaoh's followers were attributed to him personally. «جعل، یستضعف، یذبّح»

7- Allah mentioned the issue of creating division before killing and slaughter. Perhaps this is because division is the groundwork for killing, or is even more important than killing. «شیعاً... یذبّح»

8- The policymaker and the one who gives the command are also recognized as the agent and perpetrator of the crime. «انّ فرعون... یستضعف، یذبّح، یستحیى»

9- Those who create division are corruptors. «كان من المفسدین»

Yes, any society whose ruler is arrogant, whose people are divided, whose active forces are threatened, and whose resources are consumed by the affluent class, is a Pharaonic and tyrannical society.

Ayah

وَنُرِيدُ أَن نَّمُنَّ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتُضْعِفُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَنَجْعَلَهُمْ أَئِمَّةً وَنَجْعَلَهُمُ ٱلْوَٰرِثِينَ (٥)
And We desired to show favour to those who were abased in the land, and to make them imams, and to make them the heirs, (5)

Key Points

The word mann (منّت) here means a great and valuable blessing, not verbal mann (boasting about a favor) which involves recounting a blessing with the intention of humiliating others, and which is certainly an ugly and undesirable act.

There is no doubt that whenever the Divine Will is attached to the realization of an affair, that matter will certainly be actualized, and no obstacle can stand in its way. The Quran states: «انّما أمره اِذا أراد شیئاً أن یقول له كُن فیكون»(14) Indeed, His command is that whenever He wills a thing to be, then that thing will be and will be realized.

Although all blessings are from Allah, and servants are indebted to His mann (favor) for every blessing, He has specifically used the term mann for a few blessings, which naturally indicates their high importance, including:

A: The blessing of Islam. «كذلك كنتم مِن قبل فمَنّ اللّه علیكم»(15) B: The blessing of Prophethood. «لقد مَنّ اللّه على المؤمنین اذ بَعثَ فیهم رسولاً»(16) C: The blessing of Guidance. «بل اللّه یمُنّ علیكم أن هَداكم»(17) D: The blessing of the sovereignty of the believers. «و نُرید أن نمُنّ على الّذین استضعفوا فى الارض...»

In numerous narrations, Imam Mahdi عليه السلام and His government in the End of Time have been introduced as the perfect manifestation (misdaq al-atamm) of this Ayah.(18)

The issue of the sovereignty of the oppressed (mustad'afan) over the earth has been mentioned in the Quran using various phrases and expressions:

A: «وَعَداللّه الّذین آمنوا منكم و عملوا الصّالحات لیستخلفنّهم فى الارض»(19) Allah has promised succession (Khilafah) on earth to those who have believed and performed righteous deeds. B: «و لقد أهلكنا القرون من قبلكم لمّا ظلموا ... ثم جعلناكم خلائف فى الارض»(20) After We destroyed the previous oppressive nations... We made you successors on earth. C: «انّ الارض یرثها عبادى الصالحون»(21) Righteous servants shall inherit the earth. D: «و أورثنا القوم الّذین كانوا یستضعفون مشارق الارض و مغاربها»(22) We made the people who were oppressed (mustad'afan) inheritors of the eastern and western parts of the earth. E: «لنهلكنّ الظالمین . ولنسكننّكم الارض من بعدهم»(23) We shall surely destroy the oppressors, and after them, We shall settle you in the land.

The meaning of the oppressed (mustad'afan) in this Ayah, considering the passive form of the verb «استضعفوا» (were oppressed), refers to those who did not play a role in this matter themselves, but rather, arrogance (global oppression) reduced them to a state of weakness.

14) Ya Sin, 82. 15) An-Nisa, 94. 16) Al Imran, 164. 17) Al-Hujurat, 17. 18) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqaiq. 19) An-Nur, 55. 20) Yunus, 13 and 14. 21) Al-Anbiya, 105. 22) Al-A'raf, 137. 23) Ibrahim, 13 - 14.

Messages

1- The global government of the oppressed (mustad'afan) and the support for the historically wronged are the Will and Desire of Allah. «نُرید» (We desire/will).

2- In tyrannical, autocratic rule, capable forces are reduced to weakness. «استضعفوا» (were oppressed).

3- The future belongs to the oppressed (mustad'afan). «نجعلهم ائمّة...» (We shall make them leaders...).

4- Imamate (Leadership), like the Prophetic Mission (Bi'that), is a special blessing for which Allah confers His favor (mann) upon the people. Regarding the Prophet's Mission, He says: «لقد منّ اللّه على المؤمنین اذ بعث فیهم رسولاً»(24) Here, He says: «نمنّ... نجعلهم ائمّة» 24) Al Imran, 164.

Ayah

وَنُمَكِّنَ لَهُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَنُرِىَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَهَـٰمَـٰنَ وَجُنُودَهُمَا مِنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَحْذَرُونَ (٦)
and to establish them in the land, and to show Pharaoh and Haman and their hosts from them that of which they were apprehensive. (6)

Messages

1- The oppressed (mustaḍ‘afūn) should not despair, for Allah grants them power as well. «و نُمكّنَ لهم»

2- Although the main punishment for the tyrants (ṭāghūts) is in the Hereafter, they will not be safe from Divine wrath in this world either. «نُرىَ... ما كانوا یَحذرون»

3- Allah humiliates and disgraces the disbelievers at the hands of the believers. «نُرىَ... ماكانوا یَحذرون»

4- No matter how great the enemy is, it fears and dreads the believers. «ماكانوا یَحذرون»

Ayahs

وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنْ أَرْضِعِيهِ ۖ فَإِذَا خِفْتِ عَلَيْهِ فَأَلْقِيهِ فِى ٱلْيَمِّ وَلَا تَخَافِى وَلَا تَحْزَنِىٓ ۖ إِنَّا رَآدُّوهُ إِلَيْكِ وَجَاعِلُوهُ مِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۷)
We revealed to Moses’ mother, [saying], ‘Nurse him; then, when you fear for him, cast him into the river, and do not fear or grieve, for We will restore him to you and make him one of the apostles.’ (7)
فَٱلْتَقَطَهُۥٓ ءَالُ فِرْعَوْنَ لِيَكُونَ لَهُمْ عَدُوًّا وَحَزَنًا ۗ إِنَّ فِرْعَوْنَ وَهَـٰمَـٰنَ وَجُنُودَهُمَا كَانُوا۟ خَـٰطِـِٔينَ (۸)
Then Pharaoh’s kinsmen picked him up that he might be an enemy and a cause of grief to them. Indeed Pharaoh and Haman and their hosts were iniquitous. (8)

Key Points

Pharaoh had been told that a child would be born this year who, if he grew up, would overthrow him. He ordered that all newborn infants be killed; but as soon as Moses was born, Allah inspired his mother to nurse him, then place him in a chest and cast him into the river. In this Ayah, regarding the mother of Moses (عليه السلام), there are two commands, two prohibitions, and two glad tidings: Two commands: «اَرضعیه» (Nurse him) - «اَلقیه» (Cast him), Two prohibitions: «لاتخافى» (Do not fear) - «لاتحزنى» (Do not grieve), Two glad tidings: «رادّوه» (We shall return him) - «جاعلوه» (We shall make him). The word «خوف» (fear) is used regarding the possibility of danger, and the word «حُزن» (grief/sorrow) is used regarding definite anxiety. (25) The meaning of "the sea" (al-yamm) in this Ayah is the great Nile River in Egypt, which is called a sea due to its size and grandeur, and Pharaoh's palace was built on its bank. When Allah wills, even a person's enemy becomes his sanctuary and refuge. «التقطه آل فرعون» (The family of Pharaoh picked him up.) (An enemy becomes a source of good, if God wills.) If my Protector is the One Whom I know, He keeps glass safe within the embrace of stone.

25) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- During distress and dead ends, Allah is the best Guide and Protector of the believers. «اوحینا»

2- A woman can also be the recipient of Divine inspiration. «اوحینا الى امّ موسى»

3- The descent of unseen aid does not contradict the utilization of material means. Nursing and placing him in a chest do not contradict reliance (Tawakkul). «ارضعیه»

4- Under no circumstances should a mother withhold her milk from her child. «ارضعیه»

5- Divine commands contain hidden secrets that might not seem rational at first glance. «القیه فى الیم»

6- In the path of executing Divine commands, we should neither fear anything nor be anxious about any matter. «لاتخافى و لاتحزنى» (The mother of Moses had two worries: one was the fear of her child being killed, and the other was the sorrow of separation, both of which Allah compensated for in the best way, as we shall read in the subsequent verses.)

7- Whatever is given up for the sake of Allah returns in the best possible way. «القیه» (Cast him) - «رادّوه» (We shall return him), «جاعلوه من المرسلین» (We shall make him one of the Messengers). (The mother of Moses released her child by Allah's command; Allah both returned him and elevated him to the station of prophethood.)

8- Allah grants knowledge of the unseen (ghayb) to whomever He wills. «جاعلوه من المرسلین»

9- Pharaoh became the means for Moses' rescue from the river, and ultimately, Pharaoh was drowned by his hand in that very river. «التقطه آل فرعون لیكون لهم عدوّاً وحزنا» (The family of Pharaoh picked him up, that he might be an enemy and a sorrow for them). (Yes, Allah can protect a helpless infant in the river, but drown a capable adult.)

10- Human desire is ineffective against the Divine Will. «لیكون لهم عدوّاً و حزنا» (That he might be an enemy and a sorrow for them). Pharaoh thought that a gift had arrived for him; but Allah had willed to raise His enemy in his own lap.

11- Pharaoh, his minister, and his armies planned and acted, but Allah's scheme neutralized all of them. «كانوا خاطئين»

12- The planner, the advisor, the commander, and the executor are all partners in the crime in some way. «كانوا خاطئين»

Ayah

وَقَالَتِ ٱمْرَأَتُ فِرْعَوْنَ قُرَّتُ عَيْنٍ لِّى وَلَكَ ۖ لَا تَقْتُلُوهُ عَسَىٰٓ أَن يَنفَعَنَآ أَوْ نَتَّخِذَهُۥ وَلَدًا وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (۹)
Pharaoh’s wife said [to him], ‘[This infant will be] a [source of] comfort to me and to you. Do not kill him. Maybe he will benefit us, or we will adopt him as a son.’ But they were not aware. (9)

Key Points

In the life of Prophet Moses, several women played pivotal and fundamental roles: the mother of Moses, the sister of Moses, the wife of Pharaoh, and the wife of Moses. This Ayah describes the role of Pharaoh's wife in preventing his murder.

Messages

1- Women have an effective role in social affairs. «قالت امرأت فرعون» (Pharaoh's wife changed Pharaoh's mind, and neither Pharaoh's grandeur, sovereignty, nor wealth prevented her from doing so.)

2- By presenting correct and swift proposals, we should prevent the implementation of misguided plans. Before Pharaoh could express his decision, his wife suggested the correct way to handle the matter. «قالت امرأت فرعون قُرّة عین لى و لك لاتقتلوه»

3- When presenting proposals, use affectionate and pleasing words. «قُرّة عین لى ولك»

4- The hearts of all are in the hand of Allah. He can soften even the hardest hearts, such as the heart of Pharaoh. «قُرّة عین لى و لك»

5- When confronting evil deeds (munkarāt), first approach through affection, and then proceed to enjoin good and forbid evil (amr wa nahy). «قرة عین لى و لك لا تقتلوه»

6- We must not neglect verbal forbidding of evil (nahy az munkar), as it is highly effective. «لا تقتلوه» (Pharaoh's wife prevented the killing of Moses by saying «لاتقتلوه», just as one of Joseph's brothers prevented the killing of Joseph by saying «لاتقتلوا یوسف».)

7- Tyrants (Tāghūts) are devoid of even the most basic human emotions and require reminders. «لا تقتلوه»

8- We should state the benefits of enjoining good and forbidding evil so that people accept them more easily. «لاتقتلوه عسى أن یَنفعنا»

9- In a tyrannical system, the criterion for enacting or revoking laws is the whims and personal interests of the tyrants. (The law for the mass killing of male infants was established based on Pharaoh's personal interests, and based on the hope of future benefits concerning the infant brought by the water (Prophet Moses), the same law was revoked.) «لا تقتلوه عسى أن یَنفعنا»

10- Some people appear highly ambitious outwardly, but inwardly they are captive to their own internal needs. Pharaoh, who claimed: «انَاربّكم الاعلى», ultimately accepts that an infant brought by the water might prove beneficial to him. «عسى أن یَنفعنا»

11- Pharaoh was deprived of a son. Therefore, by accepting the infant brought by the water, he became more aware of his own insignificance and helplessness. «نتّخذه ولدا»

12- Sometimes the creation of affection and love between individuals is based on a divine, unseen plan, of which the parties themselves are unaware. «وهم لا یشعرون»

Ayahs

وَأَصْبَحَ فُؤَادُ أُمِّ مُوسَىٰ فَـٰرِغًا ۖ إِن كَادَتْ لَتُبْدِى بِهِۦ لَوْلَآ أَن رَّبَطْنَا عَلَىٰ قَلْبِهَا لِتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱٠)
The heart of Moses’ mother became desolate, and indeed she was about to divulge it had We not fortified her heart so that she might have faith [in Allah’s promise]. (10)
وَقَالَتْ لِأُخْتِهِۦ قُصِّيهِ ۖ فَبَصُرَتْ بِهِۦ عَن جُنُبٍ وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (۱۱)
She said to his sister, ‘Follow him.’ So she watched him from a distance, while they were not aware. (11)

Key Points

Tafsir al-Mizan interpreted the Ayah, «و اصبح فؤاد اُمّ موسى فارغا» (And the heart of Moses' mother became empty), considering the context and apparent meaning of the preceding and succeeding verses, as follows: The heart of Moses' mother was calmed by means of divine revelation, and she no longer had any fear or dread. Had it not been for that divine promise, Moses' mother would have revealed the secret due to the intensity of her anxiety. After Moses' mother placed her child in a chest and cast it into the river, she assigned Moses' sister the mission of secretly following the event. She followed the chest, away from the eyes of the government officials, until it was rescued from the water and ended up in the care of Pharaoh's wife. She continued to observe Moses until she realized that Pharaoh's courtiers were looking for wet nurses to suckle the child; however, Moses refused every breast. Finally, Moses' sister's mission concluded when she proposed her mother as a wet nurse, and the court accepted.

Messages

  1. Revealing secrets is a sign of weakness and fragility of faith. «لتبدى به لولا ان ربطنا على قلبها»
  2. The tranquility of hearts is in Allah's hand. «ربطنا على قلبها»
  3. Among the signs of faith are peace of mind and a tranquil heart. «ربطنا... لتكون من المؤمنین»
  4. What is important in Allah's sight is faith, and there is no difference between men and women. «لتكون من المؤمنین» (even though [grammatically] He should have said: «من المؤمنات»)
  5. Women and girls must also possess the necessary prudence in social matters. «قالت لاُخته قصّیه»
  6. In sensitive matters, we should delegate tasks to others. «لاُخته» In the incident of Moses (عليه السلام), the mother was under greater scrutiny, and there was a possibility that the matter would be exposed.
  7. Reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul) does not mean indifference to matters. Although Moses' mother was a woman of faith, she still sent her daughter to follow up on the incident. «من المؤمنین، قالت لاخته قُصّیه»
  8. In dealing with tyrannical and oppressive regimes, follow-up and surveillance are necessary. «قُصیّه»
  9. To preserve secrets, normalizing behavior is essential. «عن جُنُب» (Moses' sister observed her brother's chest from a distance so that this surveillance would appear very ordinary and not cause suspicion or provoke the enemy.)
  10. The believer carries out his mission away from the enemy's attention. «و هم لایشعرون»

Ayah

۞ وَحَرَّمْنَا عَلَيْهِ ٱلْمَرَاضِعَ مِن قَبْلُ فَقَالَتْ هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلَىٰٓ أَهْلِ بَيْتٍ يَكْفُلُونَهُۥ لَكُمْ وَهُمْ لَهُۥ نَـٰصِحُونَ (۱۲)
Since before We had forbidden him to be suckled by any nurse. So she said, ‘Shall I show you a household that will take care of him for you and they will be his well-wishers?’ (12)

Key Points

"Marādi‘" (مراضع) is the plural of "murḍi‘" (مرضع), meaning nursing women or wet nurses.

Messages

1- If Allah does not will it, an extensive organization like Pharaoh's regime remains incapable of providing food for a single child. "حرّمنا" (We forbade).

2- Even the most basic actions, such as a baby sucking (milk), are dependent on the Divine Will, and if He does not will it, it will not happen. "حرّمنا" (We forbade).

3- Breastfeeding (Riḍā‘) and entrusting a newborn to a wet nurse for feeding has a long and historical precedent. "المراضع" (the wet nurses).

4- When an arrogant person faces a setback, he easily accepts the guidance and suggestion of others, even an unknown individual. "فقالت هل ادلّكم" (So she said, "Shall I guide you").

5- We should present our suggestions regarding sensitive matters in a casual manner. "هل ادلّكم" (Shall I guide you).

6- One must be shrewd when dealing with the enemy. (Moses' sister did not mention the mother by name, nor did she say, "upon his mother," but rather mentioned it anonymously.) "على اهل بیتٍ" (to a household).

7- The needs of a newborn are not limited to food; he also requires a family and a center of affection. Therefore, Moses' sister spoke of comprehensive guardianship, not just feeding. "یكفلونه" (they will take care of him).

8- We should present our suggestions in such a way that the audience perceives them as being beneficial to themselves. "یكفلونه لكم" (they will take care of him for you).

9- Guardianship is valuable when accompanied by goodwill and affection. "یكفلونه لكم و هم له ناصحون" (they will take care of him for you, and they will be sincere advisors to him).

Ayah

فَرَدَدْنَـٰهُ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّهِۦ كَىْ تَقَرَّ عَيْنُهَا وَلَا تَحْزَنَ وَلِتَعْلَمَ أَنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۱۳)
That is how We restored him to his mother so that she might be comforted and not grieve, and that she might know that Allah’s promise is true, but most of them do not know. (13)

Key Points

Sometimes titles can be obstructive and hindering for a person. For example, in this incident, Moses' mother accepted to nurse her child under the title of a wet-nurse, because the title of 'mother' would have caused the murder of the child and the persecution of the mother.

Messages

1- Divine promises are never broken. In Ayah 7, Allah promised to return the infant to his mother, and in this Ayah, we read that He returned him. «انّا رادّوه الیك - رددناه الى اُمّه»

2- The infant is the light of the mother's eye. «كى تَقَرّ عینها»

3- For the people of truth, goals are important, not titles. «رددناه الى اُمّه» Ultimately, the child returned to his mother's embrace, even though this time the mother was hired as a wet-nurse; but the title is not important, the return of the infant is important.

4- Due to her deep connection with the child, the mother plays the greatest role in his life. «الى اُمّه» (The father of Moses is not mentioned here.)

5- Eventually, hardships come to an end, and separation will lead to reunion. «رددناه الى اُمّه كى تَقَرّ عینها» (After long periods of worry and grief, Allah finally returned Moses to his mother. «اِنّ مع العسر یسرا»)

6- If Allah wills, one individual can become a source of hope and the light of the eye for two opposing groups (the family of Pharaoh and the family of Moses). «قُرّة عینٍ لى ولك - تَقَرّ عینها»

7- Joy and happiness, as well as sorrow and grief, affect human eyesight. «تَقَرّ عینها» Just as we read in Ayah 84 of Surah Yusuf: the separation from Prophet Yusuf caused Prophet Ya'qub to lose his sight. «وابیَضّت عیناه من الحُزن»

8- Faith in Allah has stages and degrees. Moses' mother was a believing woman, «رَبَطنا على قلبها لتكون من المؤمنین» but a believer must also reach the stage of certainty and profound knowledge. «لتَعلم أنّ وعد اللّه حقّ»

9- The philosophy and reason behind some events is the realization of the truthfulness of Divine promises. «لتَعلم أنّ وعد اللّه حقّ»

10- Most people are superficial and have no understanding of Allah's wise secrets and deliberate plans. «اكثرهم لایعلمون»

Ayah

وَلَمَّا بَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُۥ وَٱسْتَوَىٰٓ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ حُكْمًا وَعِلْمًا ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۱٤)
When he came of age and became fully matured, We gave him judgement and knowledge, and thus do We reward the virtuous. (14)

Key Points

Ashadd (أشدّ) is derived from shiddah (شدّت), meaning becoming strong, and istawā (استوى) is derived from istiwā' (استواء), meaning the perfection and moderation of creation. The phrase ḥukman wa ‘ilman (حُكماً و عِلماً) appears three times in the Noble Qur’an, and in all instances, ḥukm (judgment/wisdom) precedes ‘ilm (knowledge). Ḥukm (حُكم) means correct understanding and the power of sound judgment, while ‘ilm (عِلم) means possessing awareness and information.

Messages

  1. The first condition for accepting responsibility is physical maturity. «و لمّا بَلَغ»
  2. True maturity is not limited only to physical growth and sexual strength, but also depends on the perfection of thought and intellect. «و لمّا بَلَغ أشدّه و استوى »
  3. Divine promises are inviolable. «جاعلوه من المرسلین - ولمّا بَلغ... آتیناه»
  4. The descent of Divine favors requires suitable conditions. «و لمّا بَلَغ ... آتیناه»
  5. Wisdom precedes knowledge. «حُكماً و علماً»
  6. Favoring the virtuous is a Divine tradition. «وكذلك»
  7. The reward of the virtuous is not limited to the Hereafter. «و كذلك نجزى المحسنین» (Moses's enjoyment of wisdom and knowledge, عليه السلام, is a Divine reward in return for his virtue.)

Ayah

وَدَخَلَ ٱلْمَدِينَةَ عَلَىٰ حِينِ غَفْلَةٍ مِّنْ أَهْلِهَا فَوَجَدَ فِيهَا رَجُلَيْنِ يَقْتَتِلَانِ هَـٰذَا مِن شِيعَتِهِۦ وَهَـٰذَا مِنْ عَدُوِّهِۦ ۖ فَٱسْتَغَـٰثَهُ ٱلَّذِى مِن شِيعَتِهِۦ عَلَى ٱلَّذِى مِنْ عَدُوِّهِۦ فَوَكَزَهُۥ مُوسَىٰ فَقَضَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ۖ قَالَ هَـٰذَا مِنْ عَمَلِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ عَدُوٌّ مُّضِلٌّ مُّبِينٌ (۱٥)
[One day] he entered the city at a time when its people were not likely to take notice. He found there two men fighting, this one from among his followers, and that one from his enemies. The one who was from his followers sought his help against him who was from his enemies. So Moses hit him with his fist, whereupon he expired. He said, ‘This is of Satan’s doing. Indeed he is clearly a misguiding enemy.’ (15)

Key Points

Apparently, before his prophethood, Prophet Moses (Musa) lived outside the city. This was either due to his revolutionary activities, causing him to reside there, or because Pharaoh's palace, where Moses عليه السلام lived, was located outside the city.

Question: If God's Prophets are infallible (ma'sum), then how is Moses' عليه السلام act of striking and killing a man justified and interpreted?

Answer: The word "هذا" (this) in the phrase "هذا من عمل الشیطان" (This is of the work of Satan) refers to the dispute and conflict between the two men, not to the action of Prophet Moses. That is, initiating this conflict is the work of Satan. Prophet Moses did not intend to kill the man; He only acted to defend a believer. For this reason, Allah did not criticize his killing, and in another place, He says: "و قَتَلت نفساً فنجّیناك من الغَمّ" (26) You killed one of the Pharaoh's people, became distressed and sorrowful, and We saved you from the distress.

26) Taha, 40.

Messages

1- Reformers of society must sometimes appear among the people anonymously and informally, without name or title. «دخل المدینة على حین غفلة من اهلها»

2- Prophet Moses had supporters and followers even before his prophethood. «مِن شیعته»

3- Although Moses was raised in Pharaoh's palace, the oppressed people considered Him their supporter and leader due to His ideology and character. «فاستغاثه»

4- Defending and supporting the oppressed is the method of the Divine Saints (Awliya Allah). «فوَكَزه موسى»

5- The Prophets were zealous, chivalrous, supportive of the oppressed, and fighters against tyranny. «فاستغاثه... فوكزه موسى»

6- Men of God would engage the enemy when necessary and utilize physical strength. «فوَكَزه موسى»

Ayah

قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّى ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِى فَٱغْفِرْ لِى فَغَفَرَ لَهُۥٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۱٦)
He said, ‘My Lord! I have wronged myself. Forgive me!’ So He forgave him. Indeed, He is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful. (16)

Key Points

| Question: What is the meaning of the phrase «ظَلمتُ نَفسى» (I wronged myself), coming from the tongue of someone who attains prophethood? Answer: The intent of "wronging oneself" here is being afflicted by the social consequences of accidental killing, not committing a sin that would contradict the status of infallibility ('ismah). This is because Allah considers Moses (Musa) to be purified (mukhlaṣ); «اِنّه كانَ مُخلَصاً»(27) And whoever attains this rank no longer commits sin, because Satan has confessed that he lacks the power to influence the purified ones (mukhlaṣīn). «فَبِعزّتِك لاُغویّنّهم اَجمَعین . اِلاّ عبادكَ منهُم المخلَصین»(28)

27) Maryam, 51. 28) Ṣād, 82 - 83.

Messages

1- In supplication (du'a), let us call upon Allah using the blessed word «ربّ» (Lord). «قال ربّ»

2- Even if a wrongful act is unintentional, one must not be heedless of its natural and consequential effects. «ظَلمتُ نفسى» (Prophet Moses entered the scene intending to save a believer and unintentionally committed homicide. He was not actually considered a sinner; however, this action complicated his mission, and this was a tribulation.)

3- Righteous individuals immediately seek forgiveness (istighfār), even for unintentional errors, and distance themselves from the consequences thereof. «فاغفرلى» (Through his seeking of forgiveness, Prophet Moses requested several things from Allah: the removal of the social consequences of his action, the alleviation of anxiety about the future, and the repulsion of the vengeful plots of the Pharaohites.)

4- The supplication of the Prophets is answered. «فَغَفرلَه»

Ayah

قَالَ رَبِّ بِمَآ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَىَّ فَلَنْ أَكُونَ ظَهِيرًا لِّلْمُجْرِمِينَ (۱۷)
He said, ‘My Lord! As You have blessed me, I will never be a supporter of the guilty.’ (17)

Key Points

Perhaps the meaning of the favor (نعمت) in the phrase "انعمت علىَّ" is divine forgiveness and pardon.

Messages

1- Physical strength is among the divine favors. «بما اَنعمتَ علىَّ» (Moses possessed a strength such that with one punch he could strike down an unbeliever.)

2- The gratitude for divine favors is avoiding oppression against others. «بما اَنعمتَ علىّ فلَن أكون ظَهیراً للمجرمین»

The gratitude for a powerful arm Is taking the hand of the weak.

3- Cooperation with sinners is also a sin. «فلن أكونَ ظَهیراً للمجرمین» (According to narrations, being pleased with the sins of others is also counted as a sin.)

Ayah

فَأَصْبَحَ فِى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ خَآئِفًا يَتَرَقَّبُ فَإِذَا ٱلَّذِى ٱسْتَنصَرَهُۥ بِٱلْأَمْسِ يَسْتَصْرِخُهُۥ ۚ قَالَ لَهُۥ مُوسَىٰٓ إِنَّكَ لَغَوِىٌّ مُّبِينٌ (۱۸)
He rose at dawn in the city, fearful and vigilant, when behold, the one who had sought his help the day before, shouted for his help [once again]. Moses said to him, ‘You are indeed clearly perverse!’ (18)

Messages

1- Not every fear is blameworthy. Fear of failing to reach one's goals or fear of the enemy achieving their desires is a praiseworthy fear. «خائفاً یَتَرقّب»

2- We must monitor the repercussions of our actions and decisions. «یَتَرقّب»

3- Despite the fact that Moses lived in Pharaoh's palace, he maintained a relationship with the people and had become known among them as a reformer. «یَستَصرخُه»

4- If taqiyyah is obligatory in a situation, and goals must remain concealed for the sake of expediency, one should not engage in daily agitation or chaos, and failure to observe this matter is considered a sin. «اِنّك لَغَوىٌّ مُبین»

5- Careless insiders must be reprimanded, and their transgressions should not be overlooked for the sake of the party or group. «اِنّك لغَوىٌّ مبین»

Ayah

فَلَمَّآ أَنْ أَرَادَ أَن يَبْطِشَ بِٱلَّذِى هُوَ عَدُوٌّ لَّهُمَا قَالَ يَـٰمُوسَىٰٓ أَتُرِيدُ أَن تَقْتُلَنِى كَمَا قَتَلْتَ نَفْسًۢا بِٱلْأَمْسِ ۖ إِن تُرِيدُ إِلَّآ أَن تَكُونَ جَبَّارًا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا تُرِيدُ أَن تَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُصْلِحِينَ (۱۹)
But when he wanted to strike him who was an enemy of both of them, he said, ‘Moses, do you want to kill me, just like the one you killed yesterday? You only want to be a tyrant in the land, and you do not desire to be one who brings about reform.’ (19)

Key Points

The word "baṭsh" (بَطش) means anger accompanied by severity and power.

Messages

1- Criticizing the wrongdoings of friends should not lead to abandoning them or failing to support their rights. (Although Prophet Moses in the previous Ayah criticized his companion with the phrase "إِنَّكَ لَغَوِيٌّ مُّبِينٌ" [Indeed, you are clearly misguided], he still decided to support him.) "أرادَ أن یَبطش" (He intended to strike).

2- Admonition is not always effective or sufficient; sometimes force and power are necessary. "أراد أن یَبطش" (He intended to strike). (Dignity ["waqār"] does not mean weakness or lethargy; Prophet Moses had street conflicts two days in a row.)

3- In an arrogant system, Pharaoh, who intentionally murders innocent infants, is called a god! But Moses, who strikes a blow against a disbelieving enemy without intending to kill him, resulting in his death, is introduced as a tyrant (jabbār)!! "تَكون جبّاراً فى الارض" (You intend to be a tyrant in the land).

Ayahs

وَجَآءَ رَجُلٌ مِّنْ أَقْصَا ٱلْمَدِينَةِ يَسْعَىٰ قَالَ يَـٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِنَّ ٱلْمَلَأَ يَأْتَمِرُونَ بِكَ لِيَقْتُلُوكَ فَٱخْرُجْ إِنِّى لَكَ مِنَ ٱلنَّـٰصِحِينَ (۲٠)
There came a man from the city outskirts, hurrying. He said, ‘Moses! The elite are indeed conspiring to kill you. So leave. I am indeed your well-wisher.’ (20)
فَخَرَجَ مِنْهَا خَآئِفًا يَتَرَقَّبُ ۖ قَالَ رَبِّ نَجِّنِى مِنَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۲۱)
So he left the city, fearful and vigilant. He said, ‘My Lord! Deliver me from the wrongdoing lot.’ (21)

Key Points

The meaning of the word «رَجُل» (man) in this Ayah is the same as the "Believer from the Family of Pharaoh" (Mumin Āl Fir'awn), after whom Surah "Mumin" (Ghafir) is named. He concealed his faith and helped Moses (Mūsā) under the guise of Taqiyyah (dissimulation).

Messages

  1. Prophet Moses had an infiltrator and supporter in Pharaoh's court. «جاء رجل»
  2. Helping the oppressor in any form is forbidden (haram); however, entering and infiltrating the regime to help the people of faith is a commendable act. «جاء رجل» (The presence of Ali ibn Yaqtin, one of the companions of Imam Kadhim عليه السلام, within the oppressive apparatus of the Abbasids was also of this nature.)
  3. Tyrants (Taghuts) do not live among the people or in the city center for their greater security and comfort. «اَقصَى المدینة»
  4. We must exert our utmost effort in protecting the lives of the leaders and elites of society. «یَسعى »
  5. Sometimes, timely, urgent, and swift reporting of news changes the destiny of a nation. «یسعى » (It is possible that if this man had not delivered the news to Moses عليه السلام and the Prophet had not left the city, he would have been killed by Pharaoh's agents.)
  6. A revolutionary person like Moses throws the front of disbelief (kufr) and its pawns into anxiety and commotion. «اِنّ المَلاء یَأتَمِرون»
  7. The entourage of the tyrants are also partners in their crimes. «یَأتَمِرون بك»
  8. Exposing conspiracies and treacherous schemes is obligatory (wajib). «یَأتمِرون بك»
  9. Reformers of society must always be prepared for migration (Hijrah) and displacement. «فَخَرج»
  10. We should value sincere warnings and accept the advice of others. «فَاخرُج - فَخَرَج»
  11. Courage does not mean making oneself accessible to the enemy. «فخَرَج»
  12. The lives of the Prophets were often accompanied by displacement and hardship. «فخَرَج»
  13. We should exert our effort and determination, but seek the result from Allah. «فخَرَج - قال رَبّ نَجّنى»
  14. Supplication (Du'a) must be accompanied by effort and action. «فخَرَج منها - قال رَبّ نَجّنى»
  15. Divine leaders seek help from Allah in all circumstances. «ربّ نَجّنى من القوم الظالمین», «ربّ اِنّى ظَلمتُ نفسى فاغفرلى»(29), «ربّ بما أنعمتَ علّى فلن أكونَ ظهیراً للمجرمین»(30) and «ربّ انّى لما أنزلتَ الىّ من خیر فَقیر»(31) 29) Qasas, 16. 30) Qasas, 17. 31) Qasas, 24.

Ayah

وَلَمَّا تَوَجَّهَ تِلْقَآءَ مَدْيَنَ قَالَ عَسَىٰ رَبِّىٓ أَن يَهْدِيَنِى سَوَآءَ ٱلسَّبِيلِ (۲۲)
And when he turned his face toward Midian, he said, ‘Maybe my Lord will show me the right way.’ (22)

Key Points

Madyan is a city in the south of Sham (Syria) and the north of Hijaz, near Tabuk, which at that time was outside the territory of Pharaoh's rule.

Messages

1- Movement, migration (Hijra), and the use of diverse tactics are prerequisites for a revolution. «و لمّا توجّه»

2- First, we must act, then we should supplicate and be hopeful. «و لمّا توجّه ... قال عسى ربّى أن یهدین»

3- Hope is commendable in all circumstances. «عسى ربّى»

4- Ups and downs are a divine tradition (Sunnah) in the path of human development. «ولمّا توجّه... قال عسى ربّى»

5- We should begin our work by seeking assistance from the Lord. «و لمّا تَوجّه... قال عسى ربّى...»

6- In environments dominated by tyranny (Taghut), where the ground is set for deviation and unwise decisions, we must seek guidance from Allah. «یَهدِیَنى سواء السبیل»

Ayahs

وَلَمَّا وَرَدَ مَآءَ مَدْيَنَ وَجَدَ عَلَيْهِ أُمَّةً مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِ يَسْقُونَ وَوَجَدَ مِن دُونِهِمُ ٱمْرَأَتَيْنِ تَذُودَانِ ۖ قَالَ مَا خَطْبُكُمَا ۖ قَالَتَا لَا نَسْقِى حَتَّىٰ يُصْدِرَ ٱلرِّعَآءُ ۖ وَأَبُونَا شَيْخٌ كَبِيرٌ (۲۳)
When he arrived at the well of Midian, he found there a throng of people watering [their flocks], and he found, besides them, two women holding back [their flock]. He said, ‘What is your business?’ They said, ‘We do not water [our flock] until the shepherds have driven out [their flocks], and our father is an aged man.’ (23)
فَسَقَىٰ لَهُمَا ثُمَّ تَوَلَّىٰٓ إِلَى ٱلظِّلِّ فَقَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّى لِمَآ أَنزَلْتَ إِلَىَّ مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَقِيرٌ (۲٤)
So he watered [their flock] for them. Then he withdrew toward the shade and said, ‘My Lord! I am indeed in need of any good You may send down to me!’ (24)

Key Points

The word tadhūdān (تذودان) is derived from the root dhawd (ذود), meaning to prevent or restrain. In this verse, the word wajada (وَجَدَ - found) is repeated twice, which indicates two separate observations, whereas if the men and women had been together, they would have been seen with a single observation.

Messages

1- We must not exploit the modesty and weakness of women. If there is no law or protection in place, many men disregard the rights of women. «اُمّة... یَسقون و وَجد من دونهم امرأتین»

2- Separation (maintaining boundaries) between men and women is a value that the daughters of Shu'ayb observed. «و وجَدَ من دونهم امرأتین»

3- The daughters of a Prophet of Allah engaged in shepherding, but they never submitted to humiliation or begging. «امرأتین تذودان»

4- Shepherding was the occupation of many Divine Prophets. «و أبونا شیخ كبیر» (If our father were not old, He would have done the shepherding Himself.)

5- Personal problems should not prevent us from supporting others. «قال ما خَطبُكما» (Musa عليه السلام, whose life was in danger and who was fleeing, did not refrain from helping and supporting others.)

6- Supporting women is a human value. «ما خَطبُكما»

7- Dialogue between a man and a woman is permissible if necessary. «ما خَطبُكما»

8- We should try to speak concisely when talking to a non-mahram (unrelated person of the opposite sex). «ما خَطبُكما»

9- We should not be indifferent to what is happening around us. «ما خطبكما»

10- A woman working outside the home is permissible, provided that:

A: The woman is not alone in the workplace. «امرأتین»

B: She does not mix freely with men. «من دونهم - لانسقى حتّى یصدر الرّعاء»

C: There is no man in the family capable of working. «أبونا شیخ كبیر»

11- If we are in an unusual situation, we should dispel any suspicion about ourselves. «و أبونا شیخ كبیر»

12- When the father and breadwinner of the family is unable to work, all children, even the daughters, are responsible. «أبونا شیخ كبیر»

13- When helping others, we should not impose conditions and should act quickly. «فسقى لهما»

As soon as Prophet Musa heard the logical reason from the two women, he immediately proceeded to fulfill their need without any further questioning or request.

14- We should serve people sincerely and ask Allah to solve our own problems. «فسقى لهما - ربّ انّى» (Musa watered the sheep of the two daughters, but to relieve his own hunger, instead of seeking help from them, he sought help from God.)

15- We should ask Allah for everything. «ربّ انّى لما انزلت الىّ من خیر فقیر»

16- In supplication (Du'a), we should not specify duties or exact instances for Allah. «ربّ انّى لما انزلت الىّ من خیر فقیر» (Although Prophet Musa was hungry, he did not ask God for a specific instance of bread or food.)

Ayah

فَجَآءَتْهُ إِحْدَىٰهُمَا تَمْشِى عَلَى ٱسْتِحْيَآءٍ قَالَتْ إِنَّ أَبِى يَدْعُوكَ لِيَجْزِيَكَ أَجْرَ مَا سَقَيْتَ لَنَا ۚ فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُۥ وَقَصَّ عَلَيْهِ ٱلْقَصَصَ قَالَ لَا تَخَفْ ۖ نَجَوْتَ مِنَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۲٥)
Then one of the two women approached him, walking bashfully. She said, ‘Indeed my father invites you to pay you the wages for watering [our flock] for us.’ So when he came to him and recounted the story to him, he said, ‘Do not be afraid. You have been delivered from the wrongdoing lot.’ (25)

Messages

1- We should pay people's rewards quickly and immediately. «فجاءته»

2- The movement of a woman outside the house must be based on modesty (Haya) and chastity. «تمشى على استحیاء» (Modesty is one of the most prominent perfections of women mentioned in the Quran.)

3- In extending an invitation, a man should invite a man, and a woman should invite a woman. «اِنّ أبى یَدعوك»

4- We should not impose ourselves as guests without an invitation. «اِنّ أبى یدعوك»

5- Whoever flees from the clutches of the tyrant (Taghut) becomes a guest in the house of Allah's Prophet. «اِنّ أبى یدعوك»

6- Hospitality towards strangers is one of the prominent characteristics of the Divine Prophets. «یدعوك»

7- We should value the services rendered by people. «لیجزیك»

8- The intention of seeking closeness (to Allah) does not contradict the appreciation shown by others. «أجر ما سقیت» (Prophet Moses performed the work for the pleasure of Allah, but Prophet Shu'ayb appreciated his efforts by paying him wages.)

9- We should interpret, analyze, and recount events for experts. «قصّ علیه القصص»

10- The ultimate outcome for the people of faith is salvation and felicity. «نَجوتَ»

11- The supplication of the Prophets is answered. «نجوتَ» Prophet Moses prayed yesterday: «ربّ نجّنى من القوم الظالمین»(32), and today He hears: «لاتخف نجوت من القوم الظالمین»

12- Being with the friends (Awliya) of Allah is a type of safety and a feeling of security. «نَجَوتَ»

32) Qasas, 21.

Ayah

قَالَتْ إِحْدَىٰهُمَا يَـٰٓأَبَتِ ٱسْتَـْٔجِرْهُ ۖ إِنَّ خَيْرَ مَنِ ٱسْتَـْٔجَرْتَ ٱلْقَوِىُّ ٱلْأَمِينُ (۲٦)
One of the two women said, ‘Father, hire him. Indeed the best you can hire is a powerful and trustworthy man.’ (26)

Key Points

Imam Reza عليه السلام said: "Prophet Shu'ayb asked his daughter: How did you realize the trustworthiness (Amanah) of this young man that you call him trustworthy (Amin)? She replied: When I conveyed your invitation to him, he told me: Guide me from behind me."(33) Lest he gaze upon my figure.

33) بحار، ج 13، ص 44.

Messages

1- Daughters also have the right to make suggestions in the family based on logic and wisdom, and they are no different from others. «قالت احداهما یا ابت استأجره»

2- Good relations between parents and children and freedom of expression within the family are a value. «یا أبت استأجره»

3- All members of the household should offer suggestions, but the final decision rests with the head of the family. «اِستأجره»

4- Chaste women seek to leave the house less often. «استأجره» (A man should be employed for difficult tasks outside the home.)

5- Work is not a disgrace; the friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah) also used to work. «استأجرت»

6- In selections, we should pay attention to the best individuals. «خیر من استأجرت»

7- For employment, two elements are necessary: capability (specialization) and trustworthiness (commitment). «القوىّ الامین»

8- Capabilities and trustworthiness must be identified and verified under normal circumstances and without the subject's awareness. «القوى الامین»

(Most people conceal their reality through artificiality, flattery, and hypocrisy; but Prophet Moses demonstrated himself in a natural setting through an act of support and chaste conduct.)

Work and Labor

  • Allah has requested from people the cultivation and development of the earth, which cannot be achieved without work and effort. «هو أنشأكم من الارض و استعمركم فیها»(34)

  • Throughout the Qur'an, emphasis is placed on righteous deeds (Amal Salih), which has a very broad meaning.

  • The Divine Prophets were farmers, shepherds, tailors, and carpenters.

  • Work in Islam is considered worship and is equivalent to Jihad.(35)

  • The prayer of the unemployed person is not answered.

  • Work is a means for training the body and soul, filling leisure time, preventing corruption and discord (Fitnah), fostering growth, genius, and innovation, promoting economic development, honor, self-sufficiency, and helping others.

  • The Qur'an states: We made the earth subservient to you, so walk upon its shoulders and eat of His provision. «هو الّذى جعل لكم الارض ذلولا فامشوا فى مناكبها و كلوا من رزقه»(36)

  • Islam holds special respect for manual labor. Observing the rights of the worker, earning his satisfaction, paying his wages quickly, paying extra beyond the determined amount, and respecting him—all of these are highly recommended in Islam. Similarly, agriculture is highly recommended, to the extent that watering a tree carries the reward of watering a believer.

  • Imam Ali عليه السلام advised his governor that farmers should not be oppressed and that their circumstances should be considered when collecting taxes.

  • Intellectual work also holds high value, to the extent that one hour of beneficial thought is better than hours of worship.

  • Underperformance and negligence in work are criticized, to the extent that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: Allah loves that whoever performs a task should perform it in the best possible manner (bi-al-ahsan).

  • The Qur'an states: So when you have finished (with a major task), then strive hard (in another major task). «فاذا فَرَغتَ فَانصَب»(37) Therefore, idleness is forbidden.

  • Indeed, idleness leads to fatigue, laziness, weariness, provides an opportunity for the penetration of Satan, creates discord (Fitnah), and spreads sin.

  • Of course, Islam has set hours and boundaries for work, and those who work during holidays have been labeled as transgressors.(38)

  • Imam Reza عليه السلام said: The believer must divide his hours into four parts: an hour for work, an hour for worship, an hour for enjoyment, and an hour for friends and attending to social affairs. Otherwise, a person will turn into a greedy element and will be subject to all the criticisms directed against greed.

  • Islam pays special attention to the quality of work, not its quantity.(39)

  • In any case, Islam has given importance to work, to the extent that we read in a Hadith: Allah dislikes the overly sleepy servant and the idle person.(40) Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: "Whoever is lazy in his worldly affairs is lazier in his affairs of the Hereafter."(41)

  • In a Hadith, we read: A poor and idle person came to the Prophet and requested help. The Prophet asked: Does anyone have an axe at home? One person said yes, and he brought the axe. The Prophet took a piece of wood that was beside Him, made it the handle of the axe, handed it over to the idle person, and said: This is the tool for work; but the effort is up to you.

  • Several points can be derived from this action:

  • The Prophets are also concerned with the lives of the people.

  • The weakest individual in society can meet with the foremost person in society.
  • Society needs cooperation and collaboration. The axe head came from one person and the wood from another; coordination, construction, and the making of the axe by the Prophet Himself.
  • To combat poverty, the tools of production must be placed at the disposal of the active forces of society.

Ayah

قَالَ إِنِّىٓ أُرِيدُ أَنْ أُنكِحَكَ إِحْدَى ٱبْنَتَىَّ هَـٰتَيْنِ عَلَىٰٓ أَن تَأْجُرَنِى ثَمَـٰنِىَ حِجَجٍ ۖ فَإِنْ أَتْمَمْتَ عَشْرًا فَمِنْ عِندِكَ ۖ وَمَآ أُرِيدُ أَنْ أَشُقَّ عَلَيْكَ ۚ سَتَجِدُنِىٓ إِن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۲۷)
He said, ‘Indeed I desire to marry you to one of these two daughters of mine, on condition that you hire yourself to me for eight years. And if you complete ten, that will be up to you, and I do not want to be hard on you. God willing, you will find me to a righteous person.’ (27)

Key Points

We can give all our actions a divine color, just as the Quran states: «صبغةاللّه و مَن أحسن من اللّه صبغة»(42) For example, whenever we wash our face, we should intend to perform Wudu (ablution); whenever we sit, we should sit facing the Qibla like the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله; we should study for the pleasure of Allah instead of for degrees and status; the clothes we buy for our spouse should be on the occasion of religious holidays like Eid al-Ghadeer or the birthday of Hadrat Zahra عليها السلام; the gift we buy for our child should be to mark the achievement of a spiritual perfection, just as in this Ayah, instead of eight years, He said: the measure of eight Hajj pilgrimages.

42) Al-Baqarah, 138.

Messages

1- If an action is performed based on chivalry and kindness toward the weak, in addition to spiritual reward, it also brings worldly recompense. «قال اِنّى اُرید أن اُنكِحُك» (Hadrat Musa, who was not even safe for his life just hours before, now gains security for his life, a wife, a home, and a job.)

2- We should learn good marriage traditions (such as being present in the daughter's home and discussing in the presence of her father) from the Prophets. «اِنّ أبى یَدعوك - اِنّى اُرید أن اُنكحك»

3- Knowing the groom before proceeding with marriage is essential. «فلما قَصّ علیه القصص - قال اِنّى اُرید أن اُنكحك» (Musa honestly recounted his story to Shu'ayb, and Shu'ayb gained confidence in Musa, after which he proposed him as a son-in-law for his daughter.)

4- If we are assured of the young man's trustworthiness, capability, and interest in work «القوىّ الامین», we should not let the lack of resources or housing prevent the marriage. «اُرید أن اُنكحك»

5- A marriage proposal coming from the father of the daughter is permissible. «اِنّى اُرید أن اُنكحك»

6- Observing the order among daughters, and the marriage of the elder daughter before the younger one, is not necessary everywhere. «احدى ابنتىّ» (Hadrat Shu'ayb told Hadrat Musa: I want to give one of these two daughters of mine in marriage to you, and he did not stipulate whether it should be the first or the second daughter.)

7- A father should not differentiate between his daughters in the matter of marriage. «اِحدى ابنتىّ هاتین»

8- The girl and the boy can meet face-to-face during the proposal. «هاتین»

9- We must take the sexual instinct seriously. Even in the house of Prophethood, for the health of the home and work environment, the matter of marriage is addressed first, and then attention is given to employment. «اُرید أن اُنكِحَك - على أن تأجرنى»

10- Marriage is accompanied by a dowry (Mahr). «على أن تأجرنى»

11- The dowry does not necessarily have to be property or wealth. Hadrat Shu'ayb told Musa: I will give my daughter in marriage to you on the condition that you work for me for eight years. «على أن تأجرنى ثمانِىَ حجج» (Of course, in the Islamic Shari'ah, we must refer to the fatwas of the Maraji' al-Taqlid [sources of emulation].)

12- We should base the timing of contracts on the timing of worship and spiritual matters. «ثمانِىَ حجج» (Instead of eight years, He said: eight Hajj pilgrimages.)

13- One way to promote good (Ma'ruf) is by honoring spiritual times and the "Days of Allah" (Ayyam Allah). «ثمانِىَ حجج»

14- The status of Prophethood does not prevent discussion regarding the amount of the dowry or the wage. «ثمانِىَ حجج»

15- The rites of Hajj also have a history in past religions. «حِجَج»

16- When discussing the dowry, the bride's father should choose the minimum acceptable amount and leave the acceptance of more to the groom's discretion. «فاِن أتمَمت عَشراً فمن عندك» Hadrat Shu'ayb told Musa: If you complete ten years instead of eight, the choice is yours.

17- Do not put the groom in difficulty regarding the amount of the dowry. «فمن عندك»

18- The bride's father should not be strict in the matter of marriage and should consider the groom's ability to pay the dowry. «ما ارید ان اَشُقّ علیك»

19- Those who are strict with their workers and subordinates are unrighteous people. «ستَجدُنى اِن شاء اللّه من الصالحین»

20- The groom must have peace of mind regarding his new relatives. «ستجدنى اِن شاء اللّه من الصالحین»

21- We should not make promises for the future without seeking help from Allah and saying Insha'Allah (if Allah wills). «اِن شاء اللّه من الصالحین»

22- Mutual trust between the groom and the bride's family is necessary. «ستَجدُنى... من الصالحین»

Importance of Marriage

We read in narrations: Marriage is the cause of preserving half of one's religion. Two units (rak'ahs) of prayer performed by a married person are better than seventy units of prayer performed by unmarried individuals. The sleep of married individuals is better than the fast of an unmarried person who stays awake in worship at night.(43)

Yes, contrary to those who consider marriage a cause of poverty, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: Marriage increases sustenance (rizq).

He also said: "Whoever abandons marriage for fear of poverty is not from us and has harbored ill-will toward Allah."(44)

We read in narrations: "Whoever takes steps for the marriage of his religious brothers will receive the special favor of Allah on the Day of Judgment."(45)

The Quran has given an emphatic command regarding forming a family and taking steps toward marriage (46) and has advised not to fear poverty, [stating that] if they are poor, Allah will enrich you from His grace and bounty.(47)

Marriage is a means of tranquility.(48) Through marriage, relatives draw closer, hearts become kind, and the ground is prepared for raising a pure generation and fostering a spirit of cooperation.(49)

We read in narrations: "Hasten toward marriage, and a girl whose time for marriage has arrived is like a ripe fruit that will spoil if not separated from the tree."(50)

Choosing a Spouse

The criteria for choosing a spouse among people are usually a few things: wealth, beauty, lineage (hasab), and ancestry (nasab).

But we read in a Hadith: «علیك بذات الدّین» (You must choose the one possessing religion). In choosing a spouse, make her belief, thought, and insight the central focus.(51) In another Hadith, we read: How often beauty leads to destruction and wealth leads to transgression.(52)

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Whenever someone comes to propose to your daughter whose religion and trustworthiness you approve of, do not refuse him, otherwise you will be afflicted by a great trial (fitnah) and corruption."(53)

Imam Hassan عليه السلام advised someone who was consulting him about his daughter's marriage: "Choose a pious groom, for if he loves your daughter, he will honor her, and if he does not love her, he will not oppress her because of the piety he possesses."(54)

We read in narrations: "Do not give your daughter in marriage to individuals who consume alcoholic beverages, those with bad character, those who lack a sound ideology, or those who have grown up in corrupt families."(55)

It should be noted that marriage is of two types: permanent (Da'im) and temporary (Muwaqqat), and specific recommendations and instructions regarding both have come in the Quran and Islamic narrations. Unfortunately, permanent marriage has become a riddle and a difficult pass due to certain illogical customs, the fault-finding of some relatives, the expectations of the bride's and groom's families, and far-fetched aspirations. Furthermore, temporary marriage has been considered so ugly and undesirable that immorality (fahsha) has taken its place.

Ayah

قَالَ ذَٰلِكَ بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَكَ ۖ أَيَّمَا ٱلْأَجَلَيْنِ قَضَيْتُ فَلَا عُدْوَٰنَ عَلَىَّ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ مَا نَقُولُ وَكِيلٌ (۲۸)
He said, ‘This will be [by consent] between you and me. Whichever of the two terms I complete, there shall be no imposition upon me, and Allah is witness over what we say.’ (28)

Key Points

The phrase «اَیّما الاَجلَینِ قَضیتُ فلاعُدوان عَلىَّ» has been interpreted in two ways: one is that the dowry (Mahr) for the daughter, whether it is eight years or ten years, neither constitutes an injustice against me; and the other meaning is that after the completion of either of these two periods, if I wish to leave you, there should be no obstacle. I will remain for the duration of this contract, and after that, the choice is mine.

Messages

1- In the marriage contract (unlike divorce), the presence of witnesses is not necessary. «بَینى و بَینك»

2- In contracts, we should consider options for ourselves and leave our hands open in certain areas. «ایّما الاَجلَین قَضیتُ فلاعدوان علىّ»

3- Faith in Allah insures and guarantees the soundness of contracts. «واللّه على ما نَقول وَكیل»

Ayah

۞ فَلَمَّا قَضَىٰ مُوسَى ٱلْأَجَلَ وَسَارَ بِأَهْلِهِۦٓ ءَانَسَ مِن جَانِبِ ٱلطُّورِ نَارًا قَالَ لِأَهْلِهِ ٱمْكُثُوٓا۟ إِنِّىٓ ءَانَسْتُ نَارًا لَّعَلِّىٓ ءَاتِيكُم مِّنْهَا بِخَبَرٍ أَوْ جَذْوَةٍ مِّنَ ٱلنَّارِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَصْطَلُونَ (۲۹)
So when Moses completed the term and set out with his family, he descried a fire on the side of the mountain. He said to his family, ‘Wait! Indeed, I descry a fire! Maybe I will bring you some news from it, or a brand of fire so that you may warm yourselves.’ (29)

Key Points

«Ānastu» (آنَستُ) means observing something that brings familiarity (uns) and tranquility. «Jadhwah» (جَذوَة) means a piece (of wood/fire), and the word «taṣṭalūn» (تَصطَلون) is derived from the root «ṣalā», meaning to warm oneself by fire. All of Prophet Moses' (عليه السلام) addresses to his wife use the plural pronoun: «umkuthū» (اُمكُثوا - stay), «ātīkum» (آتِیكم - I will bring you), «la‘allakum» (لَعلّكم - perhaps you), «taṣṭalūn» (تَصطَلون - you warm yourselves). This is perhaps because the Prophet had acquired a child or children during the ten years he lived with his wife. It is inferred from the phrases «sāra bi-ahlihi» (سارَ باَهله - he journeyed with his family), «ātīkum bi-khabar» (آتیكم بخَبَر - I will bring you news), and «la‘allakum taṣṭalūn» (لعلّكم تَصطلون - perhaps you will warm yourselves) in this Ayah, and from the phrase «ajidu ‘ala an-nāri hudā» (اَجِدُ على النّار هُدىً - I find guidance by the fire) in Ayah 10 of Surah Taha, that Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) traveled at night—a cold and dark night—during which he had also lost his way.

Messages

1- A believer is faithful to his condition and contract. «qaḍā Mūsā al-ajala» (قضى موسى الاجل)

2- Wives and children should not prevent movement, migration, and the fulfillment of responsibilities, just as a man should not abandon them; rather, they should be side-by-side and accompanying one another. «sāra bi-ahlihi» (سارَ باهله)

3- We should not take our family to a place where we lack certainty and assurance. «umkuthū» (اُمكُثوا). Seeing the fire, based on the phrase «innī ānastu nāran» (اِنّى آنَستُ ناراً - I perceive a fire), was specific to Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) and was ambiguous to him. Therefore, he asked his family to wait there so that he could go to the fire alone and return.

4- Wherever we are not certain about the fulfillment of our promise, we should not give a definitive pledge. «la‘allī ātīkum» (لعلّى آتیكم - perhaps I will bring you) - «la‘allakum taṣṭalūn» (لعلّكم تَصطلون - perhaps you will warm yourselves). (The word «la‘alla» (لعلّ) means 'perhaps,' meaning perhaps I will bring news, or perhaps I will bring a piece of fire for warming.)

5- The man is responsible for meeting the needs of his wife and family. «la‘allakum taṣṭalūn» (لعلّكم تَصطلون)

6- To reach high stations of gnosis (‘irfān), it is not necessary to separate from one's wife and children; rather, one can live an ordinary, natural, and simple life and still attain the highest degrees of gnosis. «sāra bi-ahlihi... qāla li-ahlihi... la‘allakum taṣṭalūn» (سار باهله... قال لاهله... لعلّكم تصطلون)

Ayah

فَلَمَّآ أَتَىٰهَا نُودِىَ مِن شَـٰطِئِ ٱلْوَادِ ٱلْأَيْمَنِ فِى ٱلْبُقْعَةِ ٱلْمُبَـٰرَكَةِ مِنَ ٱلشَّجَرَةِ أَن يَـٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِنِّىٓ أَنَا ٱللَّهُ رَبُّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۳٠)
When he approached it, he was called from the right bank of the valley in that blessed site from the tree: ‘Moses! Indeed I am Allah, the Lord of all the worlds!’ (30)

Key Points

The word shāṭi' means side and shore. The word wādī means river and valley. Buq'ah refers to a piece of land that differs from its neighboring lands. (56) Allah's hand is open regarding the method of revelation. Allah can inspire hearts, or send down an angel upon humans, or even create a sound from within a tree and speak to His chosen Prophet. As He says in Surah Ash-Shura: «و ما كان لبشر أن یكلّمه اللّه الاّ وحیاً او من وراء حجاب او یرسل رسولاً» (57) Allah does not speak to any human except through revelation, or from behind a veil, or by sending a messenger (angel).

56) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 57) Shura, 51.

Messages

1- Some places possess sanctity. «البقعة المباركة»

2- Where there is cause for astonishment, greater emphasis should be used. (When Moses hears a sound from a tree for the first time, he is astonished, and thus Allah's speech comes with emphasis, accompanied by innī and anā.) «اِنّى اَنا»

3- To execute His will, Allah places thoughts and sights onto a specific path. Moses was drawn in that direction seeking fire to find the way and warm his family; but Allah's goal was something else. «اِنّى آتیكم بخبر... اِنّى انَا اللّه»

Ayah

وَأَنْ أَلْقِ عَصَاكَ ۖ فَلَمَّا رَءَاهَا تَهْتَزُّ كَأَنَّهَا جَآنٌّ وَلَّىٰ مُدْبِرًا وَلَمْ يُعَقِّبْ ۚ يَـٰمُوسَىٰٓ أَقْبِلْ وَلَا تَخَفْ ۖ إِنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلْـَٔامِنِينَ (۳۱)
And: ‘Throw down your staff!’ And when he saw it wriggling as if it were a snake, he turned his back [to flee], without looking back. ‘Moses! Come forward, and do not be afraid. Indeed you are safe.’ (31)

Messages

1- Allah can create a miracle from anything. «اَلقِ عصاك... كانّها جانّ»

2- To carry out great missions, the plan must be practiced and repeated beforehand. «اَلقِ عصاك» (Prophet Moses had to first see the miracle himself so that he could repeat it before others.)

3- Prophets, in their human aspect, like other people, experienced anxiety and fear regarding certain matters. «لاتَخَف»

Ayahs

ٱسْلُكْ يَدَكَ فِى جَيْبِكَ تَخْرُجْ بَيْضَآءَ مِنْ غَيْرِ سُوٓءٍ وَٱضْمُمْ إِلَيْكَ جَنَاحَكَ مِنَ ٱلرَّهْبِ ۖ فَذَٰنِكَ بُرْهَـٰنَانِ مِن رَّبِّكَ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا فَـٰسِقِينَ (۳۲)
‘Insert your hand into your shirt. It will emerge white, without any fault, and keep your arms drawn in awe to your sides. These shall be two proofs from your Lord to Pharaoh and his elite. They are indeed a transgressing lot.’ (32)
قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّى قَتَلْتُ مِنْهُمْ نَفْسًا فَأَخَافُ أَن يَقْتُلُونِ (۳۳)
He said, ‘My Lord! Indeed, I have killed one of their men, so I fear they will kill me. (33)

Key Points

The phrase «واضمُم الیك جَناحَك من الرَّهَب» either means that when you feel fear and terror, you should place your hands on your chest or under your armpits (contrary to some who raise their hands when they are afraid), or it means to gird up your loins, become determined, and move forward. (58) It is mentioned in the Torah: when Moses put his hand into his bosom, his hand contracted leprosy and became white like snow, (59) but the Quran does not consider the whiteness of Moses' hand to be due to illness; rather, it considers it a sign of Divine power, intended to close the door to any doubt or deviation when presenting the proof. «بیضاء من غیر سوء»

58) Tafsir al-Mizan. 59) Torah, Book of Exodus, Chapter 4, Verse 6.

Messages

1- Along with the manifestation of wrath, a manifestation of grace is also necessary. «تَهتَزّ كانّها جانّ - تخرج بیضاء من غیر سوء» (Beside the terrifying serpent, the shining hand is necessary.)

2- Divine miracles have no defect or negative side effects. «من غیر سوء»

3- Guidance and invitation must have a logical and Divine backing. «برهانان من ربّك»

4- The staff turning into a serpent and the hand becoming white are signs of encouragement and punishment, warning (Inthar) and glad tidings (Bisharah), which are two essential elements in the field of education (Tarbiya). «برهانان من ربّك»

5- To reform society, one must first address the roots and sources of transgression (Fisq) and corruption. «الى فرعون و ملائه»

6- The law of retribution (Qisas) has a long history. «قَتَلتُ منهم نَفساً فَأخاف أن یَقتلون»

7- When accepting responsibility, we should state and evaluate the obstacles. «فَأخاف أن یَقتلون»

Ayahs

وَأَخِى هَـٰرُونُ هُوَ أَفْصَحُ مِنِّى لِسَانًا فَأَرْسِلْهُ مَعِىَ رِدْءًا يُصَدِّقُنِىٓ ۖ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ أَن يُكَذِّبُونِ (۳٤)
Aaron, my brother—he is more eloquent than me in speech. So send him with me as a helper to confirm me, for I fear that they will impugn me.’ (34)
قَالَ سَنَشُدُّ عَضُدَكَ بِأَخِيكَ وَنَجْعَلُ لَكُمَا سُلْطَـٰنًا فَلَا يَصِلُونَ إِلَيْكُمَا ۚ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ أَنتُمَا وَمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَكُمَا ٱلْغَـٰلِبُونَ (۳٥)
He said, ‘We will strengthen your arm by means of your brother, and invest both of you with such authority that they will not touch you. With the help of Our signs, you two, and those who follow the two of you, shall be the victors.’ (35)

Key Points

Harun (which literally means 'mountain dweller' or 'messenger') was the elder brother of Prophet Musa (Moses), one of the Prophets of the Children of Israel, and He is praised in the Quran. Prophet Musa feared two things in carrying out His mission: first, retribution for the killing, «اِنّى أخاف أن یقتلون» (Indeed, I fear they will kill me), and second, the denial of the truth, «اِنّى اَخاف أن یكذّبون» (Indeed, I fear they will deny me). When assigning heavy responsibilities, all dimensions of the person must be considered. Although Prophet Harun was older and more eloquent than Prophet Musa in terms of age and speech, Prophet Musa was made responsible and commissioned for this mission due to other qualities and merits He possessed. Although Harun was also a Divine Prophet. In the method of propagation (Da'wah), guidance, enjoining good (Amr bil Ma'ruf), and forbidding evil (Nahy anil Munkar), sometimes individuals must act in pairs or more. «ارسله معى» (Send him with me). In enjoining good and forbidding evil, the main focus is effectiveness; if a gesture works, one must gesture; if a shout works, one must shout. If it requires petitions, marches, sit-ins, strikes, gatherings, or threats and encouragement, that path must be followed, and corruption must be stopped by any means possible—in the heart, by the tongue, and through action—even if repetition is required for effect, the command and prohibition must be repeated.

Messages

1- Acknowledging the perfections of others is itself a perfection and a virtue. «هو أفصح منّى» (He is more eloquent than me). Prophet Musa, despite being an Ulul-'Azm Prophet (Prophet of Resolve), acknowledged His brother's perfection.

2- Fluent speech is one of the effective factors in attracting people and achieving successful propagation. «هو أفصح» (He is more eloquent).

3- For guidance and inviting to the truth, we should utilize the best individuals. «هو أفصح» (He is more eloquent).

4- We should employ every strength in its proper place. «هو أفصح» (Harun possessed eloquent speech, and clear communication played a fundamental role in this missionary task; thus, Prophet Musa requested his companionship from Allah.)

5- It is not necessary for officials to be superior in all perfections; rather, they must be qualified overall (من حیث المجموع). «هوأفصح منّى» (Although Musa was the primary official, Harun was stronger in certain aspects.)

6- Sometimes, to solve problems, one must proactively propose solutions and strategies to the relevant authority. «اخى هارون... فارسله» (My brother Harun... so send him).

7- The concern for planning and foresight is different from fear and terror. «انّى اخاف أن یكذّبون» (Indeed, I fear they will deny me).

8- In a Divine mission, the second person must also be appointed by Allah. «سنشدّ عضدك بأخیك» (We will strengthen your arm through your brother), just as we believe the successor to the Prophet must also be appointed by God.

9- The faith and support of relatives are highly effective in bolstering the morale of the propagator and influencing others. «أخى - یُصدّقنى» (My brother - He will confirm me).

10- The prayers and requests of the Prophets are answered. «ارسله معى - سنشدّ عضدك» (Send him with me - We will strengthen your arm). Prophet Musa requested Allah to make His brother Harun His companion and helper, and Allah answered it.

11- The best kind of brotherhood is brotherhood in affirming the truth and being the arm (support) for one another on the path of Allah. «سنشدّ عضدك باخیك» (We will strengthen your arm through your brother).

12- The victory of the Prophets is realized under the shadow of Divine signs (Ayat). «فلایَصِلون الیكما بایاتنا» (They will not reach you two because of Our signs).

13- Even the Prophets need hope and reassurance. Allah promised victory to Musa, Harun, and their followers, thereby giving them hope. «انتما ومَن اتّبعكما الغالبون» (You two and those who follow you will be the victors).

Ayah

فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُم مُّوسَىٰ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا بَيِّنَـٰتٍ قَالُوا۟ مَا هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُّفْتَرًى وَمَا سَمِعْنَا بِهَـٰذَا فِىٓ ءَابَآئِنَا ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۳٦)
When Moses brought them Our manifest signs, they said, ‘This is nothing but concocted magic. We never heard of such a thing among our forefathers.’ (36)

Messages

1- Slander is the greatest weapon of the arrogant against reformers. «ما هذا الاّ سحر مفترى»

2- The teachings of the Divine Prophets are not subject to the customs and traditions of the predecessors. «ما سمعنا بهذا فى آبائنا»

3- In accepting beliefs, logic and proof are the criteria, not the tradition of the fathers. «ما سمعنا بهذا فى آبائنا» (The lack of precedent for a thought is not proof of its incorrectness.)

4- Some people sacrifice clear logic for the sake of a dark past. «جاءهم بایاتنا بیّنات - ما سمعنا بهذا فى آبائنا الاوّلین»

Ayah

وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ رَبِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن جَآءَ بِٱلْهُدَىٰ مِنْ عِندِهِۦ وَمَن تَكُونُ لَهُۥ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلدَّارِ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ (۳۷)
Moses said, ‘My Lord knows best who brings guidance from Him, and in whose favour the outcome of that abode will be. The wrongdoers will not be felicitous.’ (37)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, it was mentioned that the Pharaohites, with complete audacity, introduced the divine miracles as sorcery and declared that they had not heard such things in the history of their forefathers. In this Ayah, Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) implicitly tells them: You are lying, because before me, individuals like Prophet Joseph (عليه السلام) held the lamp of guidance for your forefathers and promoted the religion of Abraham.

Messages

1- Against the denial of the disbelievers who call miracles sorcery, one must rely upon Allah. «ما هذا الاّ سحر... ربّى اعلم...»

2- We must neutralize baseless claims with divine slogans. «ما هذا الاّ سحر... ربّى اعلم»

3- Allah has not left any people without a leader and guide. «ربّى اعلم بمن جاء بالهُدى»

4- The guided ones have a good outcome (a blessed end). «له عاقبة الدّار»

5- Attention to the Resurrection (Ma'ad) has been at the forefront of the programs of the Prophets. «و قال موسى... مَن تكون له عاقبة الدّار»

6- Those who call the Prophets sorcerers are oppressors with a bad outcome. «لایفلح الظالمون»

Ayah

وَقَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلْمَلَأُ مَا عَلِمْتُ لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرِى فَأَوْقِدْ لِى يَـٰهَـٰمَـٰنُ عَلَى ٱلطِّينِ فَٱجْعَل لِّى صَرْحًا لَّعَلِّىٓ أَطَّلِعُ إِلَىٰٓ إِلَـٰهِ مُوسَىٰ وَإِنِّى لَأَظُنُّهُۥ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰذِبِينَ (۳۸)
Pharaoh said, ‘O [members of the] elite! I do not know of any god that you may have besides me. Haman, light for me a fire over clay, and build me a tower so that I may take a look at Moses’ god, and indeed I consider him to be a liar!’ (38)

Key Points

The word "صَرح" (ṣarḥ) means a tall building. This Ayah shows that in ancient Egypt, there were both brick kilns and they had the power to build towers.

Messages

1- The spirit of arrogance prevents the acceptance of truth. Pharaoh said: I do not know of any god for you other than myself. «ما علمت لكم من اله غیرى»

2- The arrogant impose their beliefs and ideas upon others. «ما علمت لكم من اله غیرى»

3- The slogan of the arrogant is this: Whatever I do not know, therefore, does not exist. «ما علمت...»

4- Expecting to see Allah with the physical eye is Pharaonic thinking. «اَطّلع الى اله موسى»

5- Displaying power, deceiving the masses under the guise of research and investigation, and diverting public opinion are among the methods of the arrogant. «فاوقد لى یا هامان...»

6- The arrogant consider themselves the center of everything. (Pharaoh introduced himself on every occasion) «ما علمتُ - غیرى - أوقد لى - فاجعل لى - لعلّى اطّلع - اِنّى لاظّنه»

Ayah

وَٱسْتَكْبَرَ هُوَ وَجُنُودُهُۥ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ وَظَنُّوٓا۟ أَنَّهُمْ إِلَيْنَا لَا يُرْجَعُونَ (۳۹)
