He and his hosts unduly acted arrogantly in the land, and thought they would not be brought back to Us. (39)

Key Points

| Arrogance (Istikbar) and the Arrogant (Mustakbir) * Throughout history, there have been proud and arrogant individuals who considered themselves the axis and center of everything. In this Surah, Pharaoh says: «ما علمتم لكم من اله غیرى» (I do not know of any god for you other than myself) and when the sorcerers believed in Moses, he said: «آمنتم به قبل أن آذن لكم» (Have you believed in him before I gave you permission?). Pharaoh expected that no one should hold any belief without his permission. Today, too, the Pharaohs are the superpowers who wish to consider themselves the axis of culture, politics, economics, and the masters of the world. * The arrogant sometimes destroy an individual, group, or country, or grant them favor, merely to display power. Like Nimrod, who said to Prophet Abraham: «اَنا اُحیى و اُمیت»(60) (I give life and cause death). The life and death of these people are in my hands; whomever I wish, I kill, and whomever I wish, I release from prison. * The arrogant sometimes flaunt material splendor and glamour before the people, just as Pharaoh used to say: «ألیس لى مُلك مصر وهذه الانهار تجرى من تحتى»(61) (Is the dominion of Egypt not mine, and these rivers flowing beneath me?) Do you not see that these rivers flow beneath my palace? * The arrogant sometimes compel people to submit through intimidation. Pharaoh said to the sorcerers who believed in Moses: «لاُقطّعنّ أیدیكم و أرجُلكم من خلاف»(62) (I will surely cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides). * The arrogant sometimes deceive simple-minded people through displays and self-aggrandizement. Qarun (Korah) walked through the streets and markets in such a way that the worldly people sighed and said: O, if only we had what Qarun has.(63) * The arrogant sometimes try to buy minds and thoughts through temptation. Just as Pharaoh told the sorcerers: If you discredit Moses, I will give you a great reward and make you among the close ones in my court.(64) * The arrogant sometimes shake hearts through their travels. The Quran states: Let not the movement of the disbelievers through the lands deceive you.(65) * The arrogant sometimes launch psychological warfare by belittling others and giving false accusations to the followers of the Prophets, calling them riff-raff and rabble.(66) * The arrogant sometimes captivate hearts by constructing tall and lofty buildings. Pharaoh told his minister Haman: Build me a tower so that I may ascend it and see the God of Moses!(67) Truly, how vibrant the Quran is, depicting the ugly character, behavior, and actions of the arrogant in a way that is applicable even to our time.

60) Baqarah, 258. 61) Zukhruf, 51. 62) A'raf, 124. 63) Qasas, 79. 64) A'raf, 114. 65) Aal-i Imran, 196. 66) Shu'ara, 111. 67) Ghafir, 36.

Messages

1- People follow the religion of their rulers. «استكبر هو و جنوده»

2- The criterion for the Quran's praises and criticisms is truth (Haqq). «بغیر الحقّ»

3- Negligence regarding the transient nature of the world and the assumption of immortality pave the way for arrogant behavior. «وظنّوا أنّهم الینا لا یرجعون»

4- Lack of belief in and attention to the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and accountability is the foundation for the formation of arrogance. «لایرجعون»

Ayah

فَأَخَذْنَـٰهُ وَجُنُودَهُۥ فَنَبَذْنَـٰهُمْ فِى ٱلْيَمِّ ۖ فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٤٠)
So We seized him and his hosts, and threw them into the sea. So observe how was the fate of the wrongdoers! (40)

Key Points

In this Ayah, Pharaoh and his army are likened to a worthless thing that is picked up from the ground and thrown into the sea. This degradation is because all powers are insignificant before the power and wrath of Allah. "نَبذ" (Nabdh) means throwing away worthless and insignificant things.

Messages

1- Divine punishments are not exclusive to the Hereafter. (Pharaoh was drowned in this very world) "فاخذناه" (So We seized him).

2- The followers of the oppressor share and participate in his destiny. "فاخذناه و جنوده" (So We seized him and his armies).

3- The ultimate fate of the arrogant is humiliation and wrath. "فنبذناهم" (Then We cast them away).

4- To eradicate corruption, everything that is contaminated must be destroyed. "فنبذناهم فى الیمّ" (Then We cast them into the sea).

5- We must look at historical events with an eye toward drawing lessons. "فانظر" (So look).

6- Oppressors share a common destiny. "عاقبة الظالمین" (The end of the wrongdoers). (Instead of saying the end of Pharaoh, He said: the end of all wrongdoers is like this).

7- What is important is the outcome of the matter, not its fleeting appearances. "فانظر... عاقبة الظالمین" (So look... the end of the wrongdoers).

Ayahs

وَجَعَلْنَـٰهُمْ أَئِمَّةً يَدْعُونَ إِلَى ٱلنَّارِ ۖ وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ لَا يُنصَرُونَ (٤۱)
We made them leaders who invite to the Fire, and on the Day of Resurrection they will not receive any help. (41)
وَأَتْبَعْنَـٰهُمْ فِى هَـٰذِهِ ٱلدُّنْيَا لَعْنَةً ۖ وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ هُم مِّنَ ٱلْمَقْبُوحِينَ (٤۲)
We made a curse pursue them in this world, and on the Day of Resurrection they will be among the disfigured. (42)

Key Points

The Glorious Quran, just as it uses the word 'A'immah' (leaders/Imams) for the pioneers of guidance and light, «و جعلناهم ائمّة یَهدون باَمرنا»(68), it also applies this word to the forerunners of misguidance and fire, «و جعلناهم ائمّة یدعون الى النّار». It is for this reason that on the Day of Resurrection, everyone will be summoned to the Divine Court of Justice with the leader they chose in this world. «یوم نَدعوا كلّ اُناس بامامهم»(69). Pharaoh, as one of these leaders, will also lead his people into Hellfire on that Day. «یقدم قومه یوم القیامة فاوردهم النّار»(70).

68) Anbiya, 73. 69) Isra, 71. 70) Hud, 98.

Messages

1- The spirit of arrogance (Istikbar) makes a person the leader of the inhabitants of Hell. «و جعلناهم ائمّة...»

2- The role of governments in shaping the cultural and ideological structure of society cannot be ignored. «ائمّة یدعون الى النّار»

3- The propaganda of the arrogant is, in reality, an invitation to Hellfire. «یدعون الى النّار»

4- The arrogant ones who rely on their numbers and equipment (power and resources) today will be helpless and without aid on the Day of Resurrection. «یوم القیامة لا ینصرون»

5- Divine punishments are not exclusive to the Hereafter. «و اَتبعناهم فى هذه الدّنیا»

6- The curse and condemnation of the people is a manifestation of Divine wrath. «اتبعناهم فى هذه الدّنیا لعنة» Just as the good prayers of the people and a good name are signs of Allah's favor. «و اجعل لى لسان صدق فى الاخرین»(71)

7- Cursing the oppressor is necessary even after his destruction. «اتبعناهم... لعنة»

8- Worldly evil deeds lead to ugliness (disfigurement/rejection) in the Hereafter. «یوم القیامة هم من المقبوحین»71) Shu'ara, 84.

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَآ أَهْلَكْنَا ٱلْقُرُونَ ٱلْأُولَىٰ بَصَآئِرَ لِلنَّاسِ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةً لَّعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ (٤۳)
Certainly We gave Moses the Book, after We had destroyed the former generations, as [a set of] eye-openers, guidance and mercy for mankind, so that they may take admonition. (43)

Key Points

The word "بصائر" (baṣā'ir) is the plural of "بَصیرت" (baṣīrah), meaning insight and awareness, while the word "أبصار" (abṣār) is the plural of "بَصَر" (baṣar), meaning eye.

Messages

1- With the defeat of the tyrant (Taghut), the path is paved for the righteous. «آتینا موسى الكتاب من بعد ما اهلكنا»

2- The end result for the arrogant (mustakbirūn) is destruction and annihilation. «اهلكنا القرون الاولى »

3- Religious practice must be based on insight (baṣīrah). «بصائر»

4- The sending of Books and Messengers is based on Divine Mercy. «للنّاس... رحمة»

5- Heavenly Books are the source of insight and guidance. «بصائر للناس و هدى»

6- Unless a person gains insight, he will not be guided, and unless he is guided, he will not receive Allah's grace and mercy. «بصائر للنّاس وهُدىً ورحمة»

7- Religion is part of human nature (fiṭrah), and Heavenly Books remove the dust from that nature. «یتذكّرون»

8- Despite the existence of Heavenly Books and Prophets, still, a group of people do not accept guidance. «لعلّهم یتذكّرون»

Ayah

وَمَا كُنتَ بِجَانِبِ ٱلْغَرْبِىِّ إِذْ قَضَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَى ٱلْأَمْرَ وَمَا كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلشَّـٰهِدِينَ (٤٤)
You were not on the western side when We revealed the commandments to Moses, nor were you among the witnesses. (44)

Key Points

By studying the history of those who passed away, one can live with them, even without a physical presence among them. Imam Ali عليه السلام said to his son, Imam Hassan عليه السلام: Although I have not lived the lifespan of the ancients, due to studying, reflecting upon, and scrutinizing their lives and works, it is as if I have become one of them and lived with them. (72) The phrase "ما كُنتَ" (you were not) is repeated four times in this Ayah and the subsequent Ayahs, emphasizing the unseen (ghaybi) nature of the historical accounts in the Quran.

72) Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 31.

Messages

1- The narration of the story of Prophet Moses's prophethood, with all its details, coming from the tongue of a Prophet who was not present during those times, is a sign of the truthfulness and miraculous nature (i'jāz) of the Quran. «وما كنتَ... و ما كنتَ...»

2- Some stories in the Quran are from unseen (ghaybi) reports, not from the narration of eyewitnesses. «و ما كنتَ بجانب...»

3- The western side of Mount Tur (Sinai) was the place where the Torah was revealed. «بجانب الغربى»

Ayah

وَلَـٰكِنَّآ أَنشَأْنَا قُرُونًا فَتَطَاوَلَ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْعُمُرُ ۚ وَمَا كُنتَ ثَاوِيًا فِىٓ أَهْلِ مَدْيَنَ تَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا وَلَـٰكِنَّا كُنَّا مُرْسِلِينَ (٤٥)
But We brought forth other generations and time took its toll on them. You did not dwell among the people of Midian reciting to them Our signs, but it is We who are the senders [of the apostles]. (45)

Key Points

The word "ثاویاً" (thāwiyan) is derived from the root "ثَوى" (thawā) and means resident (or abiding), just as the word "مَثوى" (mathwā), from the same root, means abode or dwelling place.

Messages

1- People in every age and time are in need of Divine knowledge. «ما كنت... یتلوا علیهم آیاتنا»

2- The passage of time diminished the effects of previous prophets and prepared the ground for the advent of the Prophet of Islam. «فتَطاول علیهم العُمُر... كنّا مرسلین»

3- The sending of prophets is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah. «كنّا مرسلین»

Ayah

وَمَا كُنتَ بِجَانِبِ ٱلطُّورِ إِذْ نَادَيْنَا وَلَـٰكِن رَّحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ لِتُنذِرَ قَوْمًا مَّآ أَتَىٰهُم مِّن نَّذِيرٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ (٤٦)
And you were not on the side of the Mount when We called out [to Moses], but [We have sent you as] a mercy from your Lord that you may warn a people to whom there did not come any warner before you, so that they may take admonition. (46)

Messages

1- Prophets do not know the unseen news, just like other people, without connection to the realm of revelation (Wahy). «ما كنت...»

2- Comparison (between ignorance and knowledge, weakness and power, poverty and richness, deficiency and perfection) is the key to self-recognition and seeking Allah. «ما كنت ... لكن رحمة من ربّك»

3- All Quranic accounts originate from the source of revelation. «ما كنت اذ نادینا»

4- The narration of the true and instructive stories of the Quran is a means for education and warning. «رحمة من ربّك لتنذر»

5- Warning the creation (Inthar) is a manifestation of Allah's mercy. «رحمة من ربّك لتنذر»

6- For the guidance of the heedless, warning (Inthar) and cautioning is more effective. «لتنذر - من نذیر» (Although the mission of the Prophets is in the form of a collection of glad tidings and warnings, since warning has a greater effect on souls afflicted by heedlessness, the Quran has paid greater attention to the issue of warning.)

7- People are free in choosing their path and belief, and there is no compulsion in accepting the invitation of the Prophets. «لتنذر... لعلّهم یتذكّرون»

8- Humans, by means of their innate nature (Fitrah), know certain matters within their conscience, but to prevent forgetfulness, they must be reminded of them. «یتذكّرون»

Ayah

وَلَوْلَآ أَن تُصِيبَهُم مُّصِيبَةٌۢ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ فَيَقُولُوا۟ رَبَّنَا لَوْلَآ أَرْسَلْتَ إِلَيْنَا رَسُولًا فَنَتَّبِعَ ءَايَـٰتِكَ وَنَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (٤۷)
And lest—if an affliction were to befall them because of what their hands have sent ahead —they should say, ‘Our Lord! Why did You not send us an apostle so that we might have followed Your signs and been among the faithful?’ (47)

Key Points

The previous Ayah considered the purpose of the Prophets' coming to be warning and admonition. This Ayah explains another purpose of the mission (Bi'that), which is the completion of the proof (Itmam al-Hujjah) and closing the path of excuses and apologies. The meaning of the Ayah is that the disbelievers say: Our misfortune and calamity are due to the corrupt deed we committed because we did not have a Prophet; if we had a Prophet, we would not have been afflicted. Allah replies: We sent Prophets to them for the completion of the proof.

Messages

  1. Many calamities and bitter events are the result of our own actions. «تصیبهم مصیبة بما قدمت ایدیهم» (A misfortune strikes them because of what their hands have put forth). Yes, good and bad deeds leave their effect even in this world.

  2. Faith in Allah and following the Prophets is the key to salvation from calamities. «تصیبهم مصیبة - لولا ارسلت»

  3. Intellect, knowledge, and innate nature (Fitrah) are not sufficient or effective on their own; rather, revelation (Wahy) is also necessary. «لولاارسلت»

  4. Punishment without prior explanation is repulsive (Qabih). «لولا ارسلت» (The mission of the Prophets is a completion of the proof so that no one can say: We were unaware and committed the transgression unknowingly.)

  5. Following and obedience are signs of true faith. «نتّبع آیاتك و نكون من المؤمنین»

Ayah

فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُمُ ٱلْحَقُّ مِنْ عِندِنَا قَالُوا۟ لَوْلَآ أُوتِىَ مِثْلَ مَآ أُوتِىَ مُوسَىٰٓ ۚ أَوَلَمْ يَكْفُرُوا۟ بِمَآ أُوتِىَ مُوسَىٰ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ قَالُوا۟ سِحْرَانِ تَظَـٰهَرَا وَقَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا بِكُلٍّ كَـٰفِرُونَ (٤۸)
But when there came to them the truth from Us, they said, ‘Why has he not been given the like of what Moses was given?’ Did they not disbelieve what Moses was given before, and said, ‘Two magicians abetting each other,’ and said, ‘Indeed we disbelieve both of them’? (48)

Key Points

Regarding which two things the phrase «سِحران تَظاهرا» refers to, many probabilities have been suggested, but considering the next Ayah, it becomes clear that the intention is the Quran and the Torah.

Messages

1- The mission of the Prophet and the Quran is truth (Haqq). «لولا ارسلت الینا رسولاً - فلما جاءهم الحقّ»

2- Deniers of the truth, instead of paying attention to its veracity, only focus on baseless and false comparisons. «جاءهم الحقّ - لولا اوتى مثل ما اوتى»

3- Rigid individuals are not willing to accept new and logical discourse. «لولا اُوتى مثل ما اُوتى موسى»

4- Sorcery and magic are the most common accusations leveled against the Prophets. «سحران» (The fault-finding deniers claimed that this Quran and that Torah are two books of sorcery that support one another.)

5- Enemies discredit the logic and word of truth (Haqq) to pave the way for their own denial. «سحران... انّا بكلّ كافرون»

Ayah

قُلْ فَأْتُوا۟ بِكِتَـٰبٍ مِّنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ هُوَ أَهْدَىٰ مِنْهُمَآ أَتَّبِعْهُ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (٤۹)
Say, ‘If you are truthful, bring some Book from Allah better in guidance than the two on them so that I may follow it.’ (49)

Messages

1- A suitable answer must be given to the frivolous claims of the deniers. «قل...»

2- To prove the truth, sometimes the method of challenge (tahaddi), confrontation, and debate must be chosen. «قل فأتوا بكتاب...»

3- Criticism alone is not enough; it must be accompanied by a plan and a proposal. «سحران تظاهرا... فأتوا بكتاب»

4- Among the distinctions of the Prophets is the challenge (tahaddi) and confrontation regarding the bringing forth of a book or a miracle. «فأتوا بكتاب - اهدى »

5- The original Torah and the Quran are the best heavenly Books for the guidance of humanity. «فأتوا بكتاب... اهدى منهما»

6- In the face of disbelief and denial, the followers of divine religions must support one another. «أهدى منهما» (more guiding than both of them) is stated, and not: «أهدى منّى» (more guiding than Me). Yes, against a common enemy who disbelieves in all heavenly Books and raises the slogan «اِنّا بكلٍ ّ كافرون» (Indeed, we are disbelievers in all of them), the Quran supports the Torah and places it alongside itself.

7- Religious leaders must present the best speech and the most logical method when facing opponents. «فأتوا بكتاب... اهدى منهما اتّبعه»

8- The criterion and standard for selection is following the best. «اهدى منهما اتّبعه»

9- In choosing a teacher, a friend, or a book, being guidance-giving is the criterion. «اهدى منهما اتّبعه»

10- Humanity is incapable of bringing forth a book superior to the Quran and the original Torah. «اِن كنتم صادقین»

Ayah

فَإِن لَّمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا۟ لَكَ فَٱعْلَمْ أَنَّمَا يَتَّبِعُونَ أَهْوَآءَهُمْ ۚ وَمَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّنِ ٱتَّبَعَ هَوَىٰهُ بِغَيْرِ هُدًى مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٥٠)
Then if they do not respond to you[r] [summons], know that they only follow their desires, and who is more astray than him who follows his desires without any guidance from Allah? Indeed Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot. (50)

Key Points

Three spiritual enemies have been enumerated for man: worldly allurements, carnal desires, and satanic whispers. Among these, the world and its allurements are like a key: moving it one way opens the door, and moving it the other way closes it. This means that one can derive good benefit from it, or one can employ it for evil purposes. Satanic whispers—although influential—do not compel man to commit sin. Furthermore, Satan has no path of infiltration or dominance over the hearts of Allah's chosen servants (Awliya Allah). However, the second enemy, which is the most effective of all, is the desires and inclinations of the self (hawa and nafs), which is considered the most dangerous enemy of man. (73)73) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Sometimes following desires (through propaganda, justifications, sophistry, and majority support) manifests in such a way that recognizing it requires insight and precision. «فاعلم»

2- Where there is no acceptance of guidance, following desires rules. «لم یستجیبوا... یتّبعون اهوائهم»

3- Deniers of the truth and followers of desires are the most misguided people. «من اضلّ ممّن اتّبع هواه»

4- True guidance is guidance that originates from infinite knowledge and wisdom. «هدىً من اللّه»

5- Following desires is oppression (zulm), and followers of desires are far from Divine guidance. «انّ اللّه لایهدى القوم الظالمین»

Ayahs

۞ وَلَقَدْ وَصَّلْنَا لَهُمُ ٱلْقَوْلَ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ (٥۱)
Certainly We have carried on this discourse for them so that they may take admonition. (51)
ٱلَّذِينَ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ مِن قَبْلِهِۦ هُم بِهِۦ يُؤْمِنُونَ (٥۲)
Those to whom We gave the Book before it are the ones who believe in it, (52)

Key Points

The word "وَصّلنا" (Waṣṣalnā) is derived from "وَصل" (waṣl), meaning to connect and join. The intent of this connection is either the continuous succession of reminders or the harmony and consistency of the content being presented. It is among the traditions (Sunan) of Allah to guide humanity, a guidance that is never interrupted, as stated: «اِنّ علینا للهُدى » (74). This guidance continues through divine verses, the Prophets and Successors علیهم السلام, and religious scholars: «وَصّلنا لهم» (We have conveyed [the word] to them). And if a region does not have access to this divine guidance, a group must migrate from that region, gain deep understanding (Tafaqquh) in religion, and then benefit the people with it. (75)

74) Layl, 12. 75) Tawbah, 122.

Messages

1- In education and training (Tarbiyah), reminders must be gradual, step-by-step, repeated, and varied. «وصّلنا» (Indeed, one should not expect reform and training with just one or two brief reminders.)

2- The verses of the Quran possess a type of consistency and continuity. «وصّلنالهم القول»

3- All divine verses, heavenly books, and religious leaders pursue a single message and goal. «وصّلنا لهم القول»

4- Man is a forgetful being and needs reminders. «یتذكّرون»

5- The true People of the Book are those who, upon seeing Islam, embrace faith. «الّذین آتیناهم الكتاب... به یؤمنون»

Ayahs

وَإِذَا يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِهِۦٓ إِنَّهُ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا مِن قَبْلِهِۦ مُسْلِمِينَ (٥۳)
and when it is recited to them, they say, ‘We believe in it. It is indeed the truth from our Lord. Indeed we were Muslims [even] before it [came].’ (53)
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يُؤْتَوْنَ أَجْرَهُم مَّرَّتَيْنِ بِمَا صَبَرُوا۟ وَيَدْرَءُونَ بِٱلْحَسَنَةِ ٱلسَّيِّئَةَ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ (٥٤)
Those will be given their reward two times for their patience. They repel evil [conduct] with good, and spend out of what We have provided them, (54)

Messages

1- Seekers of truth look for the truth and pay no attention to who says it, what race they are, or what language they speak. «اذا یُتلى ... قالوا آمنّا» ("Yutlā" is in the passive form, meaning the speaker is irrelevant.)

2- If the soul is pure, it will believe upon hearing the truth. «یُتلى ... آمنّا» (Conversely, if the ground is not prepared, there is no hope for faith even with continuous recitation.)

3- Faith is valuable only if it is based on knowledge and recognition of the truth. «آمنّا به اِنّه الحقّ»

4- The conditions and contexts of individuals differ in receiving divine favors. «یؤتون اجرهم مرّتین بما صبروا»

5- Accepting the truth requires patience (sabr) in the face of various criticisms and difficulties. «قالوا آمنّا... یؤتون أجرهم... بماصبروا»

6- Forgiving the faults of others and giving charity to them is valuable only if it is an inherent trait and characteristic of the person. «یدرؤن - ینفقون» (The present tense verb indicates permanence and continuity. Indeed, believers who possess perseverance, forgiveness, and generosity are praised.)

7- If we know that sustenance (rizq) is from Allah, giving charity becomes easy for us. «ممّا رزقناهم ینفقون»

8- The object of charity is not only wealth; one can also give charity from knowledge, ability, and reputation. «ممّا رزقناهم ینفقون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا سَمِعُوا۟ ٱللَّغْوَ أَعْرَضُوا۟ عَنْهُ وَقَالُوا۟ لَنَآ أَعْمَـٰلُنَا وَلَكُمْ أَعْمَـٰلُكُمْ سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَيْكُمْ لَا نَبْتَغِى ٱلْجَـٰهِلِينَ (٥٥)
and when they hear vain talk, they avoid it and say, ‘Our deeds belong to us, and your deeds belong to you. Peace be to you. We do not court the ignorant.’ (55)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, Allah promised a double reward to those who are patient in His way, respond to evil with good, and spend of their wealth in the way of Allah. This Ayah refers to one of the examples of patience and good conduct. "Lagw" (لَغو) means a futile or vain matter, which can manifest both in speech and in action, and believers are commanded to turn away (i'rāḍ) from it. Among the characteristics of Paradise is that futility (lagw) and vanity are absent there. «لا لغو فیها و لا تأثیم»(76)

"Mudārā" (مُدارا) [conciliation/tolerance] is yielding from a position of strength, like a father who holds his child's hand and has the power to walk fast but walks slowly. However, "Mudāhanah" (مُداهنه) [compromise/flattery] is yielding from a position of weakness, like many heads of state who, due to lack of courage, are subservient to superpowers. The Prophets and the Divine Awliyā' (saints) practice mudārā with the people, but they do not practice mudāhanah in the face of disbelief (kufr).

76) At-Tur, 23.

Messages

1- The true believer is one who not only avoids gatherings of futility (lagw) and does not listen to vain speech, but also reacts if he hears any idle talk. «و اذا سمعوا اللغو اعرضوا عنه» 2- One example of patience (ṣabr) is indifference to lagw and turning away from it. «صبروا - اعرضوا عنه» 3- Turning away (i'rāḍ) from lagw in speaking, hearing, seeing, and socializing is a perfection that has been praised in all Divine religions. «سمعوا اللغو اعرضوا عنه» (The above Ayah describes a group among the People of the Book.) 4- One of the common and simple methods of forbidding evil (nahy ‘anil-munkar) is turning away from the evil. «سمعوا... اعرضوا عنه» (We should not respond to lagw with lagw.) 5- Man is responsible for what he hears. «سمعوا... اعرضوا» 6- Decisiveness, firmness, and lack of compromise are signs of profound faith. «لنا اعمالنا و لكم اعمالكم» 7- The benefit and harm of everyone's actions return to himself. «لنا اعمالنا و لكم اعمالكم» 8- Turning away (i'rāḍ) must be noble (karīmānah). «اعرضوا... سلام علیكم» 9- Turning away must be accompanied by a warning to the people of lagw. «اعرضوا... لانبتغى الجاهلین» 10- Lagw (futility) is the fruit of ignorance (jahl), and the idle talker is ignorant (jāhil). «لانبتغى الجاهلین»

Ayah

إِنَّكَ لَا تَهْدِى مَنْ أَحْبَبْتَ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ (٥٦)
You cannot guide whomever you wish, but [it is] Allah [who] guides whomever He wishes, and He knows best those who are guided. (56)

Key Points

The content of this Ayah, which is meant to console the Noble Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله), has been repeated in other verses with different expressions and phrases. For instance, in Surah Yusuf, we read: «و ما اكثر الناس و لو حَرَصتَ بمؤمنین»(77), O Prophet! Even if you are eager for their guidance, most people will still not believe. Or in Surah Al-Baqarah, it states: «لیس علیك هداهم ولكن اللّه یهدى من یشاء»(78) The acceptance of guidance by the people is not your responsibility, and Allah guides whomever He wills.

In the fifth volume of Tafsir Durr al-Manthur (one of the Sunni commentaries), narrations are cited to the effect that on the verge of the death of Abu Talib, the father of Hadrat Ali (علیهما السلام), the Noble Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) came to him and presented Islam to him; but he did not accept, and this Ayah was revealed!!

Regardless of what the occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of the Ayah is, and whether it has any connection at all with the noble father of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (علیه السلام) or not (which is proven to be unconnected upon slight reflection, as the Ayah continues the discussion concerning a group of believers from the People of the Book in contrast to the polytheists of Mecca), we will briefly discuss these narrations and the faith of Hadrat Abu Talib.

Allamah Amini, the author of the valuable book Al-Ghadir, in the eighth volume of this book, starting from page 19, critiques and refutes all these fabricated narrations. Citing historical sources, he maintains that the narrators of such traditions were either suckling infants at the time of Hadrat Abu Talib's death or, like Abu Hurayrah, had not yet embraced Islam.

Furthermore, from page 330 to 410 of the same volume, he discusses the reasons for Hadrat Abu Talib being a believer, citing hundreds of poems by him, dozens of anecdotes, and forty traditions from the Household of the Prophet (Ahl al-Bayt) which indicate his faith in Allah and the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله علیه وآله).

Some of these reasons include:

  1. The statements of the Prophet and the infallible Imams (علیهم السلام) regarding his faith.
  2. The grief and sorrow of the Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) upon his death.
  3. The prayer of the Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) for Abu Talib from the pulpit, and his participation in his funeral procession.
  4. The Hadith of the Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) stating that "I am the intercessor for my parents and my uncle Abu Talib on the Day of Resurrection."
  5. His will concerning the support of Banu Hashim for the Prophet of Islam.
  6. His recommendation to his wife and his son Ja'far to support the Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) and pay attention to Islam and prayer (Salat).
  7. His selfless support for the Prophet, wherein, during dangerous nights, he would switch the Prophet's sleeping place with that of his son Ali (علیه السلام), thus exposing his own son to danger.
  8. The life of Fatimah bint Asad with Abu Talib, whose Islam is unquestionable, because if Abu Talib had not been a Muslim, a Muslim woman should not have been his wife.
  9. His numerous poems which attest to his affirmation of Islam.

Of course, it should not be forgotten that his only unforgivable sin is that he is the father of Ali (علیه السلام)! And what budgets his enemies spent to strike at him, fabricating narrations, and leveling the accusation of a history of polytheism against him in order to establish a balance between Abu Talib, the father of Hadrat Ali (علیهما السلام), and Abu Sufyan, the father of Mu'awiyah.

77) Yusuf, 103. 78) Al-Baqarah, 272.

Messages

  1. The duty of the Prophets is to convey the Divine messages and present the path. The acceptance or non-acceptance of the people is not related to those noble figures. «انّك لا تهدى»
  2. The Prophets possessed passion and zeal for guiding the misguided. «مَن أحبَبتَ»
  3. Guidance is the work of Allah, which only encompasses pure and prepared hearts. «ولكن اللّه یهدى»
  4. The Will of Allah is based on His Wisdom and His Knowledge. «یهدى من یشاء و هو اعلم»

Ayah

وَقَالُوٓا۟ إِن نَّتَّبِعِ ٱلْهُدَىٰ مَعَكَ نُتَخَطَّفْ مِنْ أَرْضِنَآ ۚ أَوَلَمْ نُمَكِّن لَّهُمْ حَرَمًا ءَامِنًا يُجْبَىٰٓ إِلَيْهِ ثَمَرَٰتُ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ رِّزْقًا مِّن لَّدُنَّا وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (٥۷)
They say, ‘If we follow the guidance with you, we will be forced out of our territory.’ Did We not establish a secure sanctuary for them where fruits of all kinds are brought as a provision from Us? But most of them do not know. (57)

Key Points

A group of polytheists in Mecca stated to the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله that if they accepted guidance along with him, they would quickly be driven out and displaced from their homes and dwellings, and would become destitute and deprived of their livelihood and existence. Allah refutes their excuse with three answers: The first answer, which is alluded to in this very Ayah, is that the same God Who made Mecca a secure base and a place for attracting and pouring abundant provision for you, can continue to sustain it. (This answer contains encouragement and glad tidings.) The second answer, which comes in the next Ayah, is that even if you do not believe in order to preserve your welfare and interests, what will you do about Allah's wrath? (This answer contains warning and admonition.) The third answer, which is presented in Ayah 60 of this same Surah, is: How much is the wealth and goods of this world worth that you refuse to believe because of it? Know that what is with Allah is better and more lasting. Therefore, your lack of faith has no justification or excuse.

Messages

1- Religion is not merely belief; it also includes practical programs. «نتّبع الهُدى معك»

2- The enemies' sensitivity is not only towards the path of truth and the Divine way; they object both to the path of truth and to the rightful leadership. «ان نتّبع الهُدى معك»

3- Believing does not only require knowledge; it also requires courage. «ان نتّبع الهُدى معك نتخطّف» (Yes, a group understands the truthfulness of Islam, but they refuse it in order to preserve personal interests.)

4- Man loves his homeland. «من ارضنا»

5- We should repel the fear of losing blessings by remembering Divine favors. «أولم نمكّن لهم حرماً آمناً» (Indeed, remembering blessings is a factor for reliance [Tawakkul] and the removal of worries.)

6- Economic improvement, prosperity, and growth depend on social security and tranquility. «آمناً یُحبى الیه ثمرات»

7- Security and provision (Rizq) are two guaranteed blessings of the Divine Sanctuary (Mecca). «آمناً یُحبى الیه ثمرات كلّ شى ء»

8- The superiority of faith over welfare and comfort is hidden from most people. «و لكنّ اكثرهم لا یعلمون»

Ayah

وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا مِن قَرْيَةٍۭ بَطِرَتْ مَعِيشَتَهَا ۖ فَتِلْكَ مَسَـٰكِنُهُمْ لَمْ تُسْكَن مِّنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا ۖ وَكُنَّا نَحْنُ ٱلْوَٰرِثِينَ (٥۸)
How many a town We have destroyed that exulted in its lifestyle! There lie their dwellings, uninhabited after them except by a few, and We were the [sole] inheritors. (58)

Key Points

The word "بَطِر" (baṭir) means rebellion (or transgression) and intoxication (or arrogance) resulting from an abundance of wealth and luxury. In the previous Ayah, we read that a group said to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: If we believe in you, the disbelievers of Mecca will drive us out. And Allah said: The same Power that made Mecca a place of security and abundant provision for you can also preserve His blessings for you after you embrace faith. In this Ayah, Allah says: Do not forget that We destroyed many cities that were intoxicated by blessings and immersed in luxury. What will happen to you—who refuse to believe in order to preserve your luxury and wealth—when faced with Divine wrath?! The phrase "تلك مساكنهم" (those are their dwellings) refers to the ruined dwellings of the people of 'Ad in the Al-Ahqaf region (between Yemen and Sham/Syria), or the people of Thamud in the Sodom region, which the people of Hijaz used to pass by and see with their own eyes during their trade journeys.

Messages

1- Sometimes wealth and luxury are not a source of happiness; rather, they lead to arrogance, transgression, and destruction. "كم اهلكنا... بطرت معیشتها"

2- The destiny of individuals and societies depends on their own actions. "كم اهلكنا... بطرت معیشتها"

3- The Divine tradition is the destruction of faithless affluent people. "اهلكنا من قریة بطرت"

4- The ruins and remnants of past civilizations are the best source of admonition for subsequent generations. "تلك مساكنهم" (Visiting these places is one of the educational programs.)

5- The affluent will leave their wealth behind and depart. Therefore, let us not trade eternal guidance and happiness for fleeting worldly luxury. "نحن الوارثین"

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ رَبُّكَ مُهْلِكَ ٱلْقُرَىٰ حَتَّىٰ يَبْعَثَ فِىٓ أُمِّهَا رَسُولًا يَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا ۚ وَمَا كُنَّا مُهْلِكِى ٱلْقُرَىٰٓ إِلَّا وَأَهْلُهَا ظَـٰلِمُونَ (٥۹)
Your Lord would not destroy the towns until He had raised an apostle in their mother city to recite to them Our signs. We would never destroy the towns except when their people were wrongdoers. (59)

Messages

1- Among the most important duties of the prophets is the recitation of the Divine verses upon the people. «یتلوا علیهم»

2- Mecca is the Mother of the Cities (Umm al-Qura) of Islam. «یبعث فى اُمّها رسولاً»

3- Oppressive societies are exposed to Divine wrath. «ربّك مهلكى القرى...»

4- Until the proof is established (Itmam al-Hujjah), wrath is not sent down from Allah. «ماكان ربّك مهلك القرى حتّى یبعث رسولاً»

5- Divine wrath is one of Allah's methods of training (or nurturing). (Destruction follows the word «ربّك» - Your Lord.) «ربّك مهلك القُرى »

6- Places of propagation must be in the main centers and hubs of gatherings and cities. «یبعث فى اُمّها رسولاً» (The Ayah clarifies the role of location in propagation.)

7- Failing to respond positively to the invitation of the prophets is oppression. «یتلوا علیهم... واهلها ظالمون»

8- The destruction of the oppressors in this world is a Divine tradition and program. «مهلكى القرى... اهلها ظالمون»

Ayah

وَمَآ أُوتِيتُم مِّن شَىْءٍ فَمَتَـٰعُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَزِينَتُهَا ۚ وَمَا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقَىٰٓ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ (٦٠)
Whatever things you have been given are only the wares of the life of this world and its glitter, and what is with Allah is better and more lasting. Will you not exercise your reason? (60)

Key Points

In reality, this is the third answer that Allah gives in response to the excuses of the disbelievers of Mecca (who claimed that if they embraced faith, their lives would be disrupted). [The answer is] that what you acquire through your misguided assumption of not believing is the worthless and fleeting commodity and provision of limited worldly life, while what is with Allah is better and more enduring.

Messages

1- We should not consider our wealth and possessions to be the product of our own effort and cleverness, for everything we have is from Allah. «ما اوتیتم»

2- Worldly blessings are limited; «من شى ء» but the Divine reward is unlimited and unimaginable. «ما عنداللّه خیر و ابقى »

3- If you lose the world for the sake of accepting faith, you will attain endless and everlasting blessings. «ما عنداللَّه خیرٌ و ابقى»

4- Anyone who sacrifices the enduring for the fleeting, and exchanges pure benefit for pleasures mixed with difficulties, is truly not wise. «أفلا تعقلون»

A Look at the World from the Perspective of the Quran

In the Glorious Quran, the world and worldly life are mentioned using various expressions, and people are warned against turning towards it and being deceived by it, including:

  • The world is temporary provision. «عَرَض الحیاة الدّنیا»(79)

  • The world is a mere plaything. «و ماالحیاة الدّنیا الاّ لعبٌ و لَهو»(80)

  • The world is little and insignificant. «فما متاع الحیاة الدّنیا فى الآخرة الاّ قلیل»(81)

  • Worldly life is a source of delusion and deception. «وما الحیاة الدّنیا الاّ متاع الغرور»(82)

  • Worldly life is made appealing to the disbelievers. «زیّن للّذین كفروا الحیاة الدّنیا»(83)

  • Wealth and children are the adornments of worldly life. «المال والبنون زینة الحیاة الدّنیا»(84)

  • The world is a blossom that never fully flowers for anyone. «زهرة الحیاة الدّنیا»(85)

  • Why do you sell the Hereafter for the world? «اشتروا الحیاة الدّنیا بالآخره»(86)

  • Why are you satisfied with the fleeting and limited world? «أرَضِیتم بالحیاة الدّنیا»(87)

  • Why do you rejoice in the small and transient world? «فَرِحوا بالحیوة الدّنیا»(88)

  • Why do you prefer the world over the Hereafter? «یَستحبّون الحیاة الدّنیا على الآخره»(89)

  • Why do you stare at those who possess the world? «لاتمدّنّ عینیك الى ما متّعنا به»(90)

  • Why do you only desire the world? «لم یرد الاّ الحیاة الدّنیا»(91)

  • Do the worldly not know that their abode is Hell? «و آثر الحیاة الدّنیا . فانّ الجحیم هى المأوى »(92)

Of course, all these warnings are intended to control worldly people. What is criticized in the verses and narrations is worldliness, selling the Hereafter, becoming heedless, being intoxicated [by wealth], and failing to give the rights of the poor. However, if individuals pursue the world within the framework of justice and fairness, do not neglect other perfections and the Hereafter, fulfill the rights of the deprived, and commit no injustice in acquiring or spending worldly goods, then such wealth and the world are considered Divine grace and mercy. (79) An-Nisa, 94. (80) Al-An'am, 32. (81) At-Tawbah, 38. (82) Aal-Imran, 185. (83) Al-Baqarah, 212. (84) Al-Kahf, 46. (85) Ta Ha, 131. (86) Al-Baqarah, 86. (87) At-Tawbah, 38. (88) Ar-Ra'd, 26. (89) Ibrahim, 3. (90) Al-Hijr, 88. (91) An-Najm, 29. (92) An-Nazi'at, 38 and 39.

Ayah

أَفَمَن وَعَدْنَـٰهُ وَعْدًا حَسَنًا فَهُوَ لَـٰقِيهِ كَمَن مَّتَّعْنَـٰهُ مَتَـٰعَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ثُمَّ هُوَ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ مِنَ ٱلْمُحْضَرِينَ (٦۱)
Is he to whom We have given a good promise, which he will receive, like him whom We have provided the wares of the life of this world, but who will be arraigned on the Day of Resurrection? (61)

Key Points

This Ayah, following the preceding verses, concerns those who remained in disbelief (kufr) in order to preserve their worldly life. The promise referred to in this Ayah is the same promise given in various verses of the Quran to the people of faith and righteous deeds, including Ayah 9 of Surah Al-Ma'idah: «وَعَدَ اللّهُ الّذین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحاتِ لهم مغفرةٌ و أجرٌ عظیم». The opponents of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله in early Islam were affluent and successful individuals.

Messages

1- Divine promises and rewards in the Hereafter are both great and good. «وعداً حسنا» (The word «وَعداً», which appears with tanwin, indicates the greatness of the matter, and the word «حسناً» indicates its goodness.)

2- Divine promises are definite and certain. «وعدناه ...فهو لاقیه»

3- The method of comparison is one of the best styles of propagation and education. «أفمَن وعَدناه... كمَن متّعناه» (Those who receive Divine promises are not comparable to others.)

4- The success in benefiting from worldly provisions is also in the hands of Allah. «متّعناه»

5- Worldly success is not a sign of comfort in the Hereafter. «متّعناه... من المحضرین»

6- Worldly enjoyments are subject to reckoning and accountability. «متّعناه... من المحضرین»

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «فى حلالها حساب و فى حرامها عقاب»(93) In the lawful (halal) aspects of the world there is reckoning, and in its unlawful (haram) aspects there is punishment.

7- Heedless enjoyment leads to humiliating summoning in the Hereafter. «من المحضرین» Indeed, there is no good in a pleasure whose end is the Fire. (93) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 82.

Ayahs

وَيَوْمَ يُنَادِيهِمْ فَيَقُولُ أَيْنَ شُرَكَآءِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ كُنتُمْ تَزْعُمُونَ (٦۲)
The day He will call out to them and ask, ‘Where are My ‘partners’ that you used to claim?’ (62)
قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ حَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْقَوْلُ رَبَّنَا هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ٱلَّذِينَ أَغْوَيْنَآ أَغْوَيْنَـٰهُمْ كَمَا غَوَيْنَا ۖ تَبَرَّأْنَآ إِلَيْكَ ۖ مَا كَانُوٓا۟ إِيَّانَا يَعْبُدُونَ (٦۳)
Those against whom the word had become due will say, ‘Our Lord! These are the ones whom we have perverted. We perverted them as we were perverse ourselves. We repudiate them before You: it was not us that they worshipped.’ (63)
وَقِيلَ ٱدْعُوا۟ شُرَكَآءَكُمْ فَدَعَوْهُمْ فَلَمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا۟ لَهُمْ وَرَأَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ ۚ لَوْ أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ يَهْتَدُونَ (٦٤)
It will be said, ‘Invoke your partners!’ So they will invoke them, but they will not respond to them, and they will sight the punishment, wishing they had followed guidance. (64)

Key Points

When the Resurrection is established, strange and various scenes will occur, some of which these verses refer to. One of these scenes is Allah's reproachful questioning of the polytheists. Another scene is the idols/deities expressing disgust and disavowal from those who worshipped them. And the third scene is the misguided blaming Satan, to which Satan replies: Do not blame me, but blame yourselves; «فلاتلومونى و لوموا انفسكم»(94). On the Day of Judgment, everyone tries to shift their sin onto someone else, but they receive the reply from the other side: «بل كنتم قوماً طاغین»(95). Do not shift the sin onto us; rather, you yourselves were a transgressing people. Some worshipped entities, such as Prophet Jesus (عليه السلام) and the Divine Angels, are not at fault. But those like the devils (Shayatin), the tyrants (Taghuts), and scholars who caused innovation (bid'ah) and deviation in religion are guilty, and Allah's punishment regarding them is certain.

94) Ibrahim, 22. 95) As-Saffat, 30.

Messages

  1. We must not forget the scenes of the Resurrection. «و یوم...»

  2. The court of the Resurrection is public and face-to-face. «أین شركائى»

  3. Let us not do anything that will leave us helpless and unable to answer on the Day of Judgment. «أین شركایى»

  4. Polytheism (Shirk) and reliance on anything other than Allah is mere assumption, fantasy, and mirage. «تزعمون»

  5. The cherished worldly deities will be humiliated and condemned in the Hereafter. «حقّ علیهم القول»

  6. Whoever calls others toward himself instead of toward Allah, the Divine punishment is certain for him. «حقّ علیهم القول»

  7. The worship of anything other than Allah is a deception. «اغوینا»

  8. The deceived also seek to deceive others. «اغویناهم كما غوینا»

  9. Humans are free to choose their path and can also choose deviant ways. «غوینا»

  10. False deities will one day be disgusted with their worshippers. «تبرّأنا»

  11. The reality of polytheism is the worship of desires (Hawa). «ما كانوا ایّانا یعبدون»

  12. False deities will not respond to your cries when you are in need. «فدعوهم فلم یستجیبوا» (Attachment to anything other than Allah in this world leads to deprivation of His help in the Hereafter.)

  13. The Resurrection is the Day of Regret. «لو انّهم كانوا یهتدون»

Ayahs

وَيَوْمَ يُنَادِيهِمْ فَيَقُولُ مَاذَآ أَجَبْتُمُ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (٦٥)
The day He will call out to them and say, ‘What response did you give to the apostles?’ (65)
فَعَمِيَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْأَنۢبَآءُ يَوْمَئِذٍ فَهُمْ لَا يَتَسَآءَلُونَ (٦٦)
That day all information will be withheld from them, so they will not question one another. (66)
فَأَمَّا مَن تَابَ وَءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَعَسَىٰٓ أَن يَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُفْلِحِينَ (٦۷)
As for him who repents, has faith and acts righteously, maybe he will be among the felicitous. (67)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the question was about Tawhid (Monotheism); "این شركایى - ادعوا شركائكم" (Where are My partners? - Call upon your partners). In these Ayahs, the issue of Prophethood is addressed. "ماذا أجَبتُم المرسلین" (What was your response to the Messengers?).

Messages

1- Everyone is responsible regarding the invitation of the Prophets. "ماذا أجَبتم المرسلین"

2- On the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah), the means of receiving news will be closed off, and everyone will be bewildered and helpless. "فَعَمیَت علیهم الانباء"

3- On the Day of Judgment, people will not be able to prepare an answer for the Divine Court of Justice through collaboration, consultation, or by questioning one another. "فهم لا یتسائلون"

4- In Islam, there is no deadlock. The path of return and repentance (Tawbah) is open to everyone. "مَن تاب"

5- Repentance (Tawbah) is not merely regret in the heart; it requires true faith (Iman) and righteous deeds ('Amal Salih). "تاب وآمن وعمل صالحاً"

6- The success of repentance (Tawbah) with all its conditions, its acceptance, and its continuation is not guaranteed for anyone. Therefore, a person must always be in a state of fear and hope (khawf wa raja'). (The word "عسى" means hope, meaning that with repentance, faith, and righteous deeds, there is hope for salvation.)

Ayahs

وَرَبُّكَ يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَآءُ وَيَخْتَارُ ۗ مَا كَانَ لَهُمُ ٱلْخِيَرَةُ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَ ٱللَّهِ وَتَعَـٰلَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (٦۸)
Your Lord creates whatever He wishes and chooses: they have no choice. Immaculate is Allah and exalted above [having] any partners that they ascribe [to Him]. (68)
وَرَبُّكَ يَعْلَمُ مَا تُكِنُّ صُدُورُهُمْ وَمَا يُعْلِنُونَ (٦۹)
Your Lord knows whatever their breasts conceal, and whatever they disclose. (69)
وَهُوَ ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ لَهُ ٱلْحَمْدُ فِى ٱلْأُولَىٰ وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۖ وَلَهُ ٱلْحُكْمُ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (۷٠)
He is Allah, there is no god except Him. All praise belongs to Him in this world and the Hereafter. All judgement belongs to Him, and to Him you will be brought back. (70)

Messages

1- Absolute power belongs to Allah. «یخلق ما یشاء و یختار»

2- Creation (the genesis of existence) and legislation (the law of life) are in the hands of Allah. «یخلق ما یشاء و یختار»

3- The selection of the heavenly leader is by Allah, not the people. «و یختار ما كان لهم الخیرة»

4- Only He who is the Creator of existence has the right to legislate. «یخلق ما یشاء... له الحكم»

5- Whoever accepts human law instead of Allah's law has, in reality, accepted a partner for Allah. «ما كان لهم الخیرة سبحان اللّه و تعالى عمّا یشركون»

6- Since there is no deity but Him, all praises belong exclusively to Him. «لا اله الاّ هو له الحمد»

7- Creation and selection are the work of Allah; however, He does not do vain work. «سبحان اللّه»

Ayah

قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِن جَعَلَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلَّيْلَ سَرْمَدًا إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ مَنْ إِلَـٰهٌ غَيْرُ ٱللَّهِ يَأْتِيكُم بِضِيَآءٍ ۖ أَفَلَا تَسْمَعُونَ (۷۱)
Say, ‘Tell me, if Allah were to make the night perpetual for you until the Day of Resurrection, what god other than Allah can bring you light? Will you not listen?’ (71)

Key Points

The word "sarmad" (سَرمَد) means perpetual and eternal. The phrase "أفلاتَسمعون" (Do you not hear?) is appropriate given the content of the Ayah, which concerns the night, because a person's ability to hear remains preserved at night, even if his eyes see nothing. Similarly, in the following Ayah, which discusses the day, the phrase "أفلا تبصرون" (Do you not see?) is used. Among the ways to know Allah is contemplation regarding the disappearance or change of blessings. Of course, the alternation and succession of night and day is one of the greatest blessings and Divine signs.

Messages

1- Let us learn the method of argumentation with opponents from Allah. "قل أرایتم"

2- The best signs for knowing Allah are those that are accessible to everyone, everywhere, and at all times. "قل أرایتم"

3- One of the methods of the Quran is posing awakening questions. "اِن جعل اللّه..."

4- The movement of the Earth and the creation of night and day are by Divine Will. "جعل... الیل سرمدا"

5- Nature is the most beautiful tableau and book of Divine knowledge. "الیل - ضیاء"

6- One must not imitate (blindly follow) in matters of belief. "أرأیتم - أفلا تسمعون"

7- In propagation (Da'wah), we should appeal to the conscience of the audience. "أرأیتم... من اله... أفلا تسمعون"

Ayah

قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِن جَعَلَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلنَّهَارَ سَرْمَدًا إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ مَنْ إِلَـٰهٌ غَيْرُ ٱللَّهِ يَأْتِيكُم بِلَيْلٍ تَسْكُنُونَ فِيهِ ۖ أَفَلَا تُبْصِرُونَ (۷۲)
Say, ‘Tell me, if Allah were to make the day perpetual for you until the Day of Resurrection, what god other than Allah can bring you night wherein you can rest? Will you not perceive?’ (72)

Messages

1- The system governing existence originates from Allah, and if He wills, He can transform it into another system. «اِن جعل اللّه...»

2- Allah's power is uniform regarding all phenomena. «جعل... الیل سرمداً، جعل... النهار سرمداً»

3- Night is for rest (comfort), and humanity is in need of rest. «تسكنون فیه»

4- Individuals lacking insight (basa'ir) are subject to reproach and blame. «أفلا تبصرون»

5- Polytheism (Shirk) is a consequence of lacking insight. «مَن اله غیر اللّه... أفلا تبصرون»

Ayah

وَمِن رَّحْمَتِهِۦ جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلَّيْلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ لِتَسْكُنُوا۟ فِيهِ وَلِتَبْتَغُوا۟ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (۷۳)
He has made for you the night and the day out of His mercy, so that you may rest therein and that you may seek His bounty and so that you may give thanks. (73)

Key Points

In the verses of the Quran, night always precedes day. Perhaps this is because the darkness of the night is inherent to the earth and originates from it, while the light of the day comes from the sun and is incidental to the earth.

Messages

1- The source of creation and the granting of blessings is not His need or our request, but rather His generosity and Mercy. «و من رحمته جعل»

2- The tranquility of the night is a prelude to the striving of the day. «لتسكنوا فیه و لتبتغوا»

3- Allah is the Giver, but man must strive. «من رحمته جعل... لتبتغوا»

4- Night is for comfort and day is for work and activity. «لتسكنوا فیه ولتبتغوامن فضله»

5- Whatever we attain is from His bounty; let us not boast of our own cleverness. «من فضله»

6- Material blessings must be a prelude to gratitude and the attainment of spiritual ranks. «لتسكنوا... لتبتغوا... لعلّكم تشكرون»

7- If the world is a means for growth, proximity (to Allah), and gratitude to the Divine presence, it is neither hated nor condemned. «لعلّكم تشكرون»

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ يُنَادِيهِمْ فَيَقُولُ أَيْنَ شُرَكَآءِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ كُنتُمْ تَزْعُمُونَ (۷٤)
The day He will call out to them and say, ‘Where are My ‘partners’ that you used to claim?’ (74)

Key Points

This Ayah is similar to Ayah 62 of this same Surah, which was mentioned previously.

Messages

1- The scene of the polytheists' presence on the Day of Judgment is a scene worthy of remembrance. «و یوم ینادیهم...»

2- Those who ascribe a partner to Allah should prepare themselves for accountability. «أین شركائى» (Man is responsible for his thoughts and inclinations.)

3- The Day of Judgment is the day when the futility of the worshipped deities and the helplessness of the polytheists will become manifest. «أین شركائى»

4- All worshipped deities are equal in their ineffectiveness, and all polytheists are equal in their inability to respond. «أین شركائى»

5- The Judge of the court on the Day of Judgment is the Holy Essence of Allah. «این شركائى»

6- There is no power for anyone other than Allah on the Day of Judgment. «این شركائى»

7- Allah has no partner; what they count as His partner is nothing more than assumption and illusion. «كنتم تزعمون»

Ayah

وَنَزَعْنَا مِن كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ شَهِيدًا فَقُلْنَا هَاتُوا۟ بُرْهَـٰنَكُمْ فَعَلِمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱلْحَقَّ لِلَّهِ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَفْتَرُونَ (۷٥)
We shall draw from every nation a witness and say, ‘Produce your evidence.’ Then they will know that all reality belongs to Allah and what they used to fabricate will forsake them. (75)

Key Points

The stations (or stages) of the Day of Judgment are numerous. In one station, the lips of man are sealed, and the other limbs and organs bear witness: «شَهِد علیهم سَمعُهم وأبصارهم و جُلودهم» (96) In another station, man can speak: «وَقِفوهم اِنّهم مسئولون» (97) In one place, no word is spoken to man: «لا یكلّمهم اللّه» (98), but in another place, Allah says: «هاتوا برهانكم» Although some have suggested that the intent of the Ayah is that Allah will bring forth one of the prominent individuals from every group of polytheists as a witness and spokesman and ask him: What is the proof (Burhan) for your polytheism? However, considering the narration cited under the Ayah from Imam Baqir عليه السلام (99), the intent is that in every era and time, there exists an infallible person (a Prophet or the successor of a Prophet) who is aware of people's deeds, so that he may be established as a witness to the people's conduct on the Day of Judgment.

(96) Fussilat, 20. (97) As-Saffat, 24. (98) Al-Baqarah, 174. (99) Tafsir al-Mizan, Vol. 16, p. 20.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, the witnesses arise from the nations themselves. «من كلّ امّة شهیداً»

2- The trials on the Day of Judgment are public and face-to-face. «شهیداً فقلنا»

3- We must organize our beliefs and thoughts based on firm proof and evidence. Because polytheism has no proof, and excuses will not be accepted in the Hereafter. «هاتوا برهانكم»

4- Polytheists lack logic and proof. «هاتوا برهانكم»

5- Any kind of concealment of truths is only possible in this world; in the Hereafter, when the veils are lifted, truth and reality become manifest and apparent. «فعلموا انّ الحقّ للّه»

6- Although the criminals intend to deny their deeds on the Day of Judgment, the presence of the witness drives them to a dead end. «شهیداً... فعلموا اَنّ الحقّ للّه»

7- Falsehood is bound to vanish. «ضلّ عنهم ما كانوا یفترون»

Ayah

۞ إِنَّ قَـٰرُونَ كَانَ مِن قَوْمِ مُوسَىٰ فَبَغَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ ۖ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ مِنَ ٱلْكُنُوزِ مَآ إِنَّ مَفَاتِحَهُۥ لَتَنُوٓأُ بِٱلْعُصْبَةِ أُو۟لِى ٱلْقُوَّةِ إِذْ قَالَ لَهُۥ قَوْمُهُۥ لَا تَفْرَحْ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْفَرِحِينَ (۷٦)
Korah indeed belonged to the people of Moses, but he bullied them. We had given him so much treasures that their chests indeed proved heavy for a band of stalwarts. When his people said to him, ‘Do not boast! Indeed Allah does not like the boasters. (76)

Key Points

In the commentary Majma' al-Bayan, a tradition is narrated stating that Qarun was the cousin (son of the maternal aunt) of Prophet Musa (Moses) and a knowledgeable man, possessing unparalleled skill in reading the Torah. He was initially among the companions of Musa عليه السلام and part of the group of seventy individuals who accompanied him to Mount Tur for supplication (munajat) there; but due to acquiring immense wealth, he raised the banner of opposition and was ultimately subjected to divine wrath and punishment. Throughout his struggle, Prophet Musa was engaged with three main axes of corruption and transgression: One was the axis of power and force, spearheaded by Pharaoh. The second was the lever of wealth and gold (zar), symbolized by Qarun. And the third was the factor of deception and hypocrisy (tazwir), led by Samiri. In other words, Prophet Musa was grappling with the ominous triangle of force (zur), gold (zar), and deception (tazwir).

Messages

1- Mentioning historical examples serves as a lesson for future generations. «اِنّ قارون...»

2- A good past is not proof of a good future, nor does it justify overlooking present deviations. «كان من قوم موسى »

3- Being related to a Prophet is not, by itself, a factor for success and salvation. «كان من قوم موسى »

4- The existence of deviant relatives should not diminish the status of righteous and reforming individuals. «اِنّ قارون كان من قوم موسى »

5- Immense wealth is a means to power, and power is a factor leading to transgression and indulgence. «الكنوز - فبَغى علیهم - لاتفرح»

6- Wealth should not make us arrogant or intoxicated (with pride). «لاتفرح»

7- Enjoining good and forbidding evil (Nahy 'an al-Munkar) against the wealthy is obligatory, even for the deprived class. «قال له قومه لاتفرح»

8- Intoxicated (with pride) individuals are deprived of divine love. «اِنّ اللّه لایحبّ الفرحین» (Indeed, wealth and possessions are not signs of popularity with Allah.)

Ayah

وَٱبْتَغِ فِيمَآ ءَاتَىٰكَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلدَّارَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ ۖ وَلَا تَنسَ نَصِيبَكَ مِنَ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَأَحْسِن كَمَآ أَحْسَنَ ٱللَّهُ إِلَيْكَ ۖ وَلَا تَبْغِ ٱلْفَسَادَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ (۷۷)
Seek the abode of the Hereafter by means of what Allah has given you, while not forgetting your share of this world. Be good [to others] just as Allah has been good to you, and do not try to cause corruption in the land. Indeed Allah does not like the agents of corruption.’ (77)

Key Points

Imam Ali عليه السلام, in interpreting the phrase “lā tansa naṣībaka mina d-dunyā” (do not forget your share of the world), said: “It means utilize your health, strength, leisure, youth, and vitality for the Hereafter.” (100) This Ayah refers to several educational instructions and guidelines: A: Paying attention to the Hereafter, alongside benefiting from the world. B: Showing kindness to others, considering Allah’s favors upon humanity. C: Avoiding corruption, considering “إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ الْمُفْسِدِينَ” (Indeed, Allah does not love the corrupters).

100) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The world is the farm of the Hereafter. “وَابْتَغِ فِيمَا آتَاكَ اللَّهُ الدَّارَ الْآخِرَةَ” (Qarun was told: Seek the abode of the Hereafter through these means.)

2- The wealth of the rich belongs to Allah. “آتَاكَ اللَّهُ”

3- The Hereafter must be sought earnestly, although the world should not be forgotten either. “ابْتَغِ” (seek) - “لَا تَنْسَ” (do not forget).

4- Property and wealth can become a means for achieving felicity in the Hereafter. “وَابْتَغِ فِيمَا آتَاكَ اللَّهُ الدَّارَ الْآخِرَةَ”

5- Admonishing the wealthy is a commendable act. “وَابْتَغِ...”

6- Everyone should be content with their share and portion, and spend the rest for the Hereafter. “وَابْتَغِ... وَلَا تَنْسَ نَصِيبَكَ...”

7- In admonition, we must also pay attention to natural needs. “لَا تَنْسَ نَصِيبَكَ”

8- Unlimited wealth does not lead to unlimited enjoyment. Everyone’s portion is limited and specified. “نَصِيبَكَ”

9- Seeking the Hereafter is achieved through kindness to others. “وَابْتَغِ... وَأَحْسِنْ”

10- To invite others to kindness, reminding them of Allah’s kindness toward them is effective. “أَحْسِنْ كَمَا أَحْسَنَ اللَّهُ إِلَيْكَ”

11- Faithless capitalists are exposed to corruption. “لَا تَبْغِ الْفَسَادَ”

Ayah

قَالَ إِنَّمَآ أُوتِيتُهُۥ عَلَىٰ عِلْمٍ عِندِىٓ ۚ أَوَلَمْ يَعْلَمْ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَدْ أَهْلَكَ مِن قَبْلِهِۦ مِنَ ٱلْقُرُونِ مَنْ هُوَ أَشَدُّ مِنْهُ قُوَّةً وَأَكْثَرُ جَمْعًا ۚ وَلَا يُسْـَٔلُ عَن ذُنُوبِهِمُ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ (۷۸)
He said, ‘I have indeed been given [all] this because of the knowledge that I have.’ Did he not know that Allah had already destroyed before him some of the generations who were more powerful than him and greater in amassing [wealth]? The guilty will not be questioned about their sins. (78)

Key Points

Question: Although we read in many verses of the Quran that the questioning on the Day of Judgment will be about everyone: «فلنسئلنّ الّذین أُرسل الیهم و لنسئلنّ المرسلین» (101), about everything: «ولتسئلنّ عما كنتم تعلمون» (102), about every condition: «اِنْ تُبدوا ما فى أنفسكم أو تُخفوه یُحاسبكم به اللّه» (103), and about every place: «اِنْ تَك مثقال حبّة مِن خَردَلٍ فَتكن فى صَخرةٍ أو فى السموات أو فى الارض یأتِ بها اللّه» (104), why then does this verse state: «لا یسئل عن ذنوبهم المجرمون»?! Answer: The verses that indicate questioning relate to the Day of Judgment; however, the latter verse refers to the time when divine wrath descends in this world, at which point there is no longer any respite for questioning and answering.

(101) Al-A'raf, 6. (102) An-Nahl, 93. (103) Al-Baqarah, 284. (104) Luqman, 16.

Messages

1- Academic arrogance sometimes makes a person so self-centered that he no longer accepts the role of anyone or anything else. «انّما أوتیته على عِلمٍ عندى»

2- Boasting about one's own knowledge is the characteristic of Qarun. «على عِلمٍ عندى»

3- We must consider wealth and power as divine favor, not the product of our own knowledge and effort. (People said: It is Allah Who gave you wealth; Qarun said: I acquired it myself based on knowledge.) «احسن اللّه - على عِلمٍ عندى»

4- Familiarity with history is the best lesson to learn. «أولم یعلم... من قبله»

5- The eradication of the arrogant is among the divine traditions of Allah. (Power and wealth do not prevent the descent of divine wrath.) «أهلك من قبله»

6- Power and wealth do not bring about happiness. «أهلك... أشدّ منه قوّة و أكثر جمعا»

7- There is always a greater power above any power. «من هو أشدّ منه»

8- Warning! If divine wrath arrives, there will be no further opportunity for questioning, answering, or arguing. «لایُسئل عن ذنوبهم المجرمون»

Ayah

فَخَرَجَ عَلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦ فِى زِينَتِهِۦ ۖ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ يُرِيدُونَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا يَـٰلَيْتَ لَنَا مِثْلَ مَآ أُوتِىَ قَـٰرُونُ إِنَّهُۥ لَذُو حَظٍّ عَظِيمٍ (۷۹)
So he emerged before his people in his finery. Those who desired the life of the world said, ‘We wish we had like what Korah has been given! Indeed he is greatly fortunate.’ (79)

Messages

1- Power and wealth in the hands of heedless people lead to boasting, showing off, and extravagance. «فخرج على قومه فى زینته»

2- Flaunting wealth is a characteristic of Qarun. «فخرج على قومه فى زینته»

3- Those who possess Qarun's characteristics consider themselves superior to others. «على قومه»

4- The elitism of rulers can change the culture of the people. «على قومه فى زینته... یا لیت لنا...»

5- The beautiful appearances of the world are a trap for attracting and deviating the short-sighted. «قال الّذین یریدون الحیاة الدّنیا»

6- The wish to become Qarun is the wish of foolish materialists who desire the worldly life. «یریدون الحیاة الدّنیا یالیت لنا...»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ وَيْلَكُمْ ثَوَابُ ٱللَّهِ خَيْرٌ لِّمَنْ ءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا وَلَا يُلَقَّىٰهَآ إِلَّا ٱلصَّـٰبِرُونَ (۸٠)
Those who were given knowledge said [to them], ‘Woe to you! Allah’s reward is better for someone who has faith and acts righteously, and no one will receive it except the patient.’ (80)

Key Points

Qarun considered himself knowledgeable and believed he acquired wealth through his own knowledge: «اوتیته على علم عندى». However, Allah says in this Ayah that true knowledge is not compatible with hoarding wealth.

Messages

1- Those infatuated with the world deserve blame and reproach. «ویلكم»

2- Allah praises the learned among the Children of Israel for confronting those infatuated with the world. «قال الّذین اوتوا العلم ویلكم...»

3- True knowledge guides man toward the Hereafter, piety (Taqwa), and righteous deeds. «قال الّذین اوتوا العلم ویلكم ثواب اللّه خیر»

4- An aware scholar is one whom the glitter of the world does not attract, and who scorns the materialists. «قال الّذین اوتوا العلم ویلكم ثواب اللّه خیر»

5- Scholars must warn people against the love of the world. «قال الّذین اوتوا العلم ویلكم ثواب اللّه خیر»

6- If we forbid someone from something, we should, if necessary and possible, offer him something better than it. «ثواب اللّه خیر»

7- Faith and righteous deeds bring happiness only when a person is steadfast upon them. «آمن و عمل صالحاً و لا یلقّاها الاّ الصّابرون»

Ayah

فَخَسَفْنَا بِهِۦ وَبِدَارِهِ ٱلْأَرْضَ فَمَا كَانَ لَهُۥ مِن فِئَةٍ يَنصُرُونَهُۥ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُنتَصِرِينَ (۸۱)
So We caused the earth to swallow him and his house, and he had no party that might protect him from Allah, nor could he rescue himself. (81)

Key Points

Man can move in two arcs: an ascending arc that leads to ascension (Mi'raj) and a descending arc that sinks him to the earth.

Messages

1- The result of oppression (zulm) is destruction. «فبغى علیهم... فخسفنا به»

2- The earth is sometimes commissioned by Allah's wrath. «فخسفنا به و بداره الارض»

3- The ultimate end of hoarding wealth, stinginess, arrogance, and pride is destruction. «فخسفنا به و بداره الارض»

4- Wealth is not a means of salvation. «ما كان من المنتصرین»

Ayah

وَأَصْبَحَ ٱلَّذِينَ تَمَنَّوْا۟ مَكَانَهُۥ بِٱلْأَمْسِ يَقُولُونَ وَيْكَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦ وَيَقْدِرُ ۖ لَوْلَآ أَن مَّنَّ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْنَا لَخَسَفَ بِنَا ۖ وَيْكَأَنَّهُۥ لَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ (۸۲)
By dawn those who longed to be in his place the day before were saying, ‘Don’t you see that Allah expands the provision for whomever He wishes of His servants, and tightens it? Had Allah not shown us favour, He might have made the earth swallow us too. Don’t you see that the faithless do not prosper?’ (82)

Messages

1- When dealing with issues, we should not judge hastily. (Those who yesterday regretted, wishing they were like Qarun, today say: How good it is that we were not like him.) «تمنّوا مكانه بالامس یقولون - و یكأنّه...»

2- We should never wish to be like the arrogant and heedless wealthy people. «تمنّوا مكانه... و یكأنّ»

3- Instead of wishing for the wealth of others, we should be content with the divine provisions. «تمنّوا مكانه... یبسط الرّزق... و یقدر»

4- Not all wishes are beneficial. «تمنّوا مكانه»

5- Sometimes, the non-acceptance of a prayer and the non-fulfillment of a wish is the greatest favor and grace of Allah upon man. «تمنّوا مكانه... منّ اللّه علینا»

6- Events cause the awakening of innate natures (fitrah) and the change of false perceptions and desires. «لولا أن منّ اللّه علینا»

7- Wealth that a person does not recognize as being from Allah and for His sake leads him to disbelief (kufr). «لایفلح الكافرون»

Ayah

تِلْكَ ٱلدَّارُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةُ نَجْعَلُهَا لِلَّذِينَ لَا يُرِيدُونَ عُلُوًّا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا فَسَادًا ۚ وَٱلْعَـٰقِبَةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ (۸۳)
This is the abode of the Hereafter, which We shall grant to those who do not desire to domineer in the earth nor to cause corruption, and the outcome will be in favour of the Godwary. (83)

Key Points

This Ayah serves as the resolution to the story of Qarun, [stating] that any form of wealth accumulation and seeking superiority leads to destruction in this world and misery in the Hereafter. Based on narrations, Imam Ali عليه السلام used to recite this Ayah for the merchants in the marketplace.(105) Imam Khomeini قدس سره, the founder of the Islamic Republic, also paid attention to it at the end of his ethics lessons for the students (Tullab) and scholars (Fudhala) of the Qom Seminary. We read in a Hadith that if someone considers himself superior to another merely because his shoelace is better, he is counted among those who desire superiority (uluww) on earth. How many people, in preparing housing, transportation, clothing, speech, marriage, and naming their children, act in a way that makes them visible in society and draws people's attention to them? According to the narration, these individuals desire superiority on earth and are deprived of Paradise.

105) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan and Ruh al-Bayan.

Messages

1- The abode of the Hereafter is truly magnificent. (The word «تلك» [tilka] is used to express grandeur.)

2- Any form of seeking superiority is forbidden. «لا یریدون علوّاً»

3- The source of corruption (fasad) is seeking superiority. «لایریدون علوّاً... و لا فسادا»

4- A pious person (muttaqi) is one who does not intend to boast or seek superiority over others. «لایریدون علوّاً... والعاقبة للمتّقین»

Ayah

مَن جَآءَ بِٱلْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُۥ خَيْرٌ مِّنْهَا ۖ وَمَن جَآءَ بِٱلسَّيِّئَةِ فَلَا يُجْزَى ٱلَّذِينَ عَمِلُوا۟ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ إِلَّا مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۸٤)
Whoever brings virtue shall receive [a reward] better than it, but whoever brings vice—those who commit misdeeds shall not be requited except for what they used to do. (84)

Messages

1- Good deeds are desirable and commendable, regardless of who performs them or in what quantity. «مَن جاء بالحسنة»

2- More important than performing a good deed is ensuring its safe arrival to the scene of the Resurrection. «جاء بالحسنة» (How many people perform good deeds, but destroy them through sin, showing favor, self-admiration, or belittling others, thus failing to deliver their good deed to the Day of Judgment.)

3- Humans are free in choosing their path in life. «مَن جاء بالحسنة - مَن جاء بالسیئة»

4- Even in speech, we should begin our discourse with good things. (The word «الحسنة» comes first, followed by «السیئة»).

5- In the system of Allah's complete grace, the reward for good deeds is greater than the deeds themselves, even if they do not reach the stage of action and remain in the state of thought and intention. However, the punishment for a bad deed is only equal to the bad deed, and only if it passes the stage of thought and intention and takes the form of action. «عملوا السیئات»

6- We should not forget the good deeds of others, even if it is a simple act, «الحسنة». But we should only punish evildoers when they insist on their bad deeds. «ما كانوا یعملون»

7- Allah gives the reward for good deeds through His grace, but He deals with the punishment of evildoers through His justice. «فله خیرٌ منها - لایجزى ... الاّ ما كانوا یعملون»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِى فَرَضَ عَلَيْكَ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ لَرَآدُّكَ إِلَىٰ مَعَادٍ ۚ قُل رَّبِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ مَن جَآءَ بِٱلْهُدَىٰ وَمَنْ هُوَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (۸٥)
Indeed He who has revealed to you the Quran will surely restore you to the place of return. Say, ‘My Lord knows best him who brings guidance and him who is in manifest error.’ (85)

Key Points

In Ayah 7, it was mentioned that Allah inspired the mother of Moses to cast her child into the sea and not to worry, for "We shall surely return him to you," «القیه فى الیمّ - انّا رادّوه الیك». In this Ayah too, Allah promises the Prophet of Islam that He will return him to his birthplace. «لرادّك الى معاد» Yes, the same power that returned Moses to his mother will also return you (after the Hijra/migration) to Mecca. Regarding what meaning «معاد» (Ma'ad) refers to and what its specific instance (مصداق) is, different views have been presented. Some consider it "Mecca," the birthplace of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, and view this Ayah as a divine prophecy, meaning: O Prophet! One day this very Mecca will be conquered by your hands, and you will return to it. Others have interpreted it as the Prophet's Maqam Mahmud (the Station of Praise, the station of intercession/Shafa'at), and a group has interpreted it as the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) and Paradise. However, narrations consider the meaning of Ma'ad in this Ayah to be the Raj'ah (return) of the Prophet of Islam to the world. Raj'ah (رَجعت) is one of our definite beliefs, derived from many Ayahs and narrations. This means that Allah will return a group of His chosen saints (Awliya) to the world before the Day of Judgment.

Messages

1- The conveyance of the Quran led to the rebellion of the disbelievers and the migration (Hijra) of the Prophet; however, the God of the Quran will return the Prophet to his birthplace again. «لرادّك الى معاد»

2- Recitation, propagation, and acting upon the Quran is obligatory upon the Prophet himself before anyone else. «فرض علیك القرآن»

3- The expression that the Quran is an obligation (faridah) is a sign of its importance and high status. «فرض علیك القرآن»

4- In the context of explanation (or discourse), we should begin with the positive points first. «مَن جاء بالهُدى - مَن هو فى ضلال»

Ayah

وَمَا كُنتَ تَرْجُوٓا۟ أَن يُلْقَىٰٓ إِلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبُ إِلَّا رَحْمَةً مِّن رَّبِّكَ ۖ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ ظَهِيرًا لِّلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۸٦)
You did not expect that the Book would be delivered to you; but it was a mercy from your Lord. So do not be ever an advocate of the faithless. (86)

Key Points

Two similarities between Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) and Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله) are observed in the verses of this Surah: 1. Prophet Moses went towards the fire hoping to obtain it, and reached the light, (فلمّا اتاها نودى...) (Ayah 30). The Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله) also went to the Cave of Hira for the purpose of worship and became acquainted with the heavenly revelation. (ما كنت ترجوا ان...) 2. In Ayah 17, Prophet Moses stated: (ربّ بما اَنعمتَ علىّ فلن اكون ظهیراً للمجرمین) "My Lord! Because of the favor You have bestowed upon me, I will never be a supporter of the criminals." In this Ayah too, Allah says to the Noble Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله): (یُلقى الیك الكتاب... فلا تكوننّ ظهیراً للكافرین) "Because of the heavenly Book that has been revealed to you, never be a supporter of the disbelievers."

Messages

1- Even the Prophets, despite their purity of soul and perfection in servitude, did not expect to receive revelation; revelation was a Divine mercy. (ما كنت ترجوا ...الاّ رحمة من ربّك)

2- The appointment of Prophets and the descent of heavenly Books are among the affairs of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah). (رحمة من ربّك)

3- Disassociation and avoidance of supporting the disbelievers is so important that it is considered the gratitude (shukranah) for the descent of revelation. (یُلقى الیك الكتاب ... فلا تكوننّ...)

4- Any form of support for disbelief (kufr) and backing of crime is forbidden. (فلاتكوننّ ظهیراً للكافرین)

Ayah

وَلَا يَصُدُّنَّكَ عَنْ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ بَعْدَ إِذْ أُنزِلَتْ إِلَيْكَ ۖ وَٱدْعُ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (۸۷)
Do not ever let them bar you from Allah’s signs after they have been sent down to you. Invite to your Lord, and never be one of the polytheists. (87)

Key Points

Prophethood (Bi'that) has stages: A: Receiving revelation. «یُلقى الیك الكتاب» B: Dissociation from the disbelievers. «فلاتكوننّ ظهیراً» C: Firmness in the task. «لایصدنّك» D: Inviting others. «اُدع الى ربّك» E: Sincerity in belief and action. «لاتكوننّ من المشركین»

Attention to Tawhid (monotheism) and the negation of polytheism (Shirk) and disbelief (Kufr) is so important that Allah repeatedly warns the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله about it in these verses. Most sentences concerning this issue in the recent verses use the heavy Nun of emphasis (Nun at-Tawkeed ath-Thaqilah) and are expressed with an emphatic tone. «لاتكوننَّ - لایصدّنّك - لاتكوننّ»

Messages

1- Compromising in the conveyance of revelation is a form of strengthening disbelief (Kufr). «فلاتكوننّ ظهیراً للكافرین و لایصدنّك»

2- Enemies have plots even for the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله himself. «لایصدّنّك»

3- Heavenly leaders also need Divine reminders. «لایصدّنّك»

4- The mission (Risalah) must be accompanied by dignity and decisiveness. «لایصدّنّك... بعد اذ انزلت»

5- Knowledge and awareness make the responsibility heavier. «بعد اذ انزلت الیك»

6- Success in inviting to the truth belongs to the one who is decisive and impenetrable. «لایصدّنّك... اُدع الى ربّك»

7- The invitation must be sincere and consider nothing except the pleasure of Allah. «اُدع الى ربّك و لاتكوننّ من المشركین» Indeed, whoever fails to invite to God due to consideration for others has entered the sphere of polytheism (Shirk).

8- The invitation must be towards God, not oneself. «اُدع الى ربّك»

9- Eliminating polytheism (Shirk) and fighting disbelief (Kufr) is the forefront of the Prophets' activities and one of Allah's most serious emphases. «لاتكوننّ من المشركین»

Ayah

وَلَا تَدْعُ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَـٰهًا ءَاخَرَ ۘ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ كُلُّ شَىْءٍ هَالِكٌ إِلَّا وَجْهَهُۥ ۚ لَهُ ٱلْحُكْمُ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (۸۸)
And do not invoke another god besides Allah; there is no god except Him. Everything is to perish except His Face. All judgement belongs to Him, and to Him you will be brought back. (88)

Key Points

The "Wahhabi" sect claims that anyone who calls upon other than Allah becomes a polytheist (Mushrik), because Allah has said: «فلاتدع مع اللّه أحداً» (106). Therefore, those who call upon the friends of Allah (Awliya) in Tawassul (seeking intercession) are polytheists! However, this Ayah clarifies that a polytheist is one who calls upon other than Allah as another god: «لاتدع مع اللّه الهاً آخر». It is self-evident that Shias do not call upon any figure as a god; rather, they call upon them as someone who holds honor (prestige/standing) with Allah—an honor that has been granted to them by Allah, such as the Prophets and the Divine Awliya, and not every imaginary intercessor or person of prestige. This is because idolaters also imagined prestige for their idols in their world of fantasy.

In this Ayah, the removal of polytheism (Shirk) is expressed through various phrases: A: Do not call upon another god alongside "Allah." B: There is no deity except Allah. C: Everything except Him is perishable. D: Sovereignty belongs solely to Him. E: You shall return only to Him.

The King of Saudi Arabia hosts scholars from Islamic sects every year on the day of Eid al-Adha. In one of those years, Allamah Sayyid Sharaf al-Din Jabal Amili (one of the prominent Lebanese scholars) was invited to the gathering. As soon as the Allamah entered the session, he presented the King with a Quran that had a leather cover. The King took it and kissed it. The Allamah said: "You are a polytheist!" The King was upset, asking why he was leveling such an accusation. The Allamah said: "Because you kissed leather, which is the skin of an animal, and showing respect to animal skin is polytheism!" The King said: "I do not kiss every piece of skin. My shoe is made of leather and animal skin, but I never kiss it. This piece of leather has become the cover of the Quran." The Allamah replied: "We also do not kiss every piece of iron; we kiss the iron that forms the box, the Dharih (shrine enclosure), the fortress, or the window of the grave of the Prophet or the infallible Imams علیهم السلام."

Yes, polytheism (Shirk) is when we elevate someone or something against Allah and attribute independent power to them, whereas the Shia do not consider the power of the friends of Allah to be independent, but rather a power dependent on Divine power. And if they build domes and shrines (Bar-gah), it is to announce to the people that a man of Monotheism (Tawhid) is buried here. The person buried here was martyred in the way of Allah and became a sacrifice and annihilation for Monotheism. Thus, the dome and shrine signify a center beneath which the cry of Monotheism is raised, not a center standing against the mosque.

Imam Reza علیه السلام said: "The 'Face of Allah' (Wajh Allah) refers to the Prophet of Allah and His proofs on earth, through whom attention is directed toward Allah, His religion, and His knowledge." (107) In the Supplication of Nudbah, concerning Imam al-Zaman علیه السلام, we read: «أین وجه اللّه الّذى یُؤتى » (Where is the Face of Allah through whom [He] is approached?) and we also read: «أین وجه الّذى یتوجّه الاولیاء» (Where is the Face toward whom the Awliya turn?).

106) الجنّ, 18. 107) توحید صدوق, p. 117.

Messages

1- The true Monotheist is one who is free from all deities, superpowers, 'isms,' and tyrants (Taghut). «لاتدع مع اللّه الهاً آخر»

2- Let us not become attached to what is perishable, nor make ourselves captive to them. «كلّ شى ءٍ هالك الاّ وجهه» Just as Prophet Abraham said: «لا اُحبّ الآفلین» (I do not love those that set). (108)

3- Sovereignty belongs exclusively to Allah. «له الحكم»

4- The true object of worship must meet several conditions: A: He must not be perishable. «كلّ شى ء هالك الا وجهه» B: He must be the Sovereign over existence. «له الحكم» C: The ultimate return of all must be to Him. «الیه ترجعون»

5- Death is not annihilation; it is a return to the Origin. «الیه ترجعون»

O Lord! Let us taste the sweetness of reciting, reflecting upon, and taking heed from the Quran. O Lord! Illuminate the lamp of our hearts with the light of the Quran and make our existence a source of illumination. O Lord! Make our conduct and speech arise from the Quran and the path (Sirah) and tradition (Sunnah) of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله and his infallible Household علیهم السلام.

108) انعام, 76.
